exploration.description: Science ships staffed by a Scientist survey celestial bodies, revealing resources, habitable planets, and enabling colonization. Surveying can trigger anomalies (special events) and archaeological sites. Two early strategies: survey sequentially from the home system to minimize Influence costs, or race to chokepoints to block rivals. Science ships can be set to auto‑explore. First contacts with other empires begin when a fleet or science ship enters their space, requiring an envoy to complete.

jobs.name: Jobs
jobs.description: Jobs are where pops produce resources. They are divided into strata; higher strata fill first. Most jobs are created by districts and buildings. For non-Wilderness empires, pops work jobs with efficiency bonuses from traits, and will switch jobs based on suitability. Each job has a slider to cap the number of workers.

technology.description: Stellaris technology is divided into 3 research areas — Physics, Society, and Engineering — each corresponding to its own research resource. Technologies belong to one of 13 subcategories and are organized into 6 tiers (T0 to T5). Instead of a tech tree, the game uses a card‑shuffle approach: when a research project completes, a semi‑random hand of 3 (or more) available techs is drawn from a hidden deck. Base cost is modified by galaxy settings and by empire size (research cost increases by 0.2% per point of empire size above 100).

physics.computing: research labs, research speed, science ships, ship computers, point‑defense, sensors, espionage, Cosmogenesis advancement technologies
physics.field_manipulation: energy production, shields, cloaking devices, astral rifts, some strategic resources, cosmic storm manipulation
physics.particles: ship reactors, energy weapons, FTL travel, some strategic resources

society.biology: food production, genetic modification, leader lifespan and enhancement, biological ship components, army types
society.military_theory: fleet command limit, naval capacity, army buildings and stats, claim cost
society.new_worlds: habitability, terraforming, blocker removal, starbase capacity, upgraded capital buildings
society.statecraft: unity and edicts, leaders, envoys, diplomacy, upgraded capital buildings, various buildings for unity, trade and amenities
society.psionics: exotic technologies unlocked in mid to late game, associated with psionic ascension
society.archaeostudies: archaeology, minor artifacts, archaeotechnologies (requires Ancient Relics DLC)

engineering.industry: mineral production, robotics, building construction, cosmic storm protection
engineering.voidcraft: ship types and hulls, starbases, strike craft, megastructures

card_system.description: Technologies appear as a hand of 3 cards (research alternatives) drawn from a deck of all currently valid options. A new hand is drawn when a project completes. Higher tiers require 6 technologies from the preceding tier in the same area. Each tech has a weight that determines its chance to appear; weight can be modified by ethics, civics, scientist traits, etc. A tech that appeared in the previous hand has its weight halved in the next draw.
card_system.tier_requirement: 6 technologies from preceding tier per area
card_system.permanent_alternatives: Some techs can become golden‑bordered permanent alternatives (always appear) from debris analysis, special projects, anomalies, archaeological sites, or events.

weight_modifiers.rare_techs_scientific_inspiration: ×2 (with federation perk and another member has the tech)

research_progress.formula_monthly: Monthly research progress = Research resource produced × Σ Research speed modifiers + min[Research resource stored, Research resource produced]
research_progress.formula_time: Research time = Σ (Research cost − Partial research done) / Monthly research progress
research_progress.stored_research: Unused research output accumulates as stored research. When a project is active, stored research is consumed each month at a rate equal to current output (effectively doubling progress) until the stored amount falls below output; any remainder is consumed in the final month.
research_progress.empire_size_penalty: Each point of empire size above 100 increases research cost by +0.2%.

debris.description: Debris spawns when at least one ship from a hostile fleet is destroyed. It can provide progress on most components, weapons, and defenses, but not ship upgrades, hulls, or repeatable technologies. Each component debris gives 10% progress on the corresponding tech (or 10% on the prerequisite if unresearched). If no component can be spawned, debris yields a small amount of stored research instead. Ships destroyed by an empire with the Enigmatic Engineering ascension perk leave no debris.

salvaging.policy: Scavenge yields no research, instead gives resources equal to 10% of ship build cost. Research & Salvage Debris (requires Scavengers or Refurbishment Division civic) gives both research and resources, plus a chance to salvage a ship of equal hull size.
salvaging.salvage_chance_formula: % chance = (10 + additional salvage chance + combo bonus) × Salvager trait level

salvage_modifiers.master_salvager_trait: +15% salvage chance (and applies ×1.5 multiplier on total Salvager trait level)
salvage_modifiers.combo_bonus: +5% if Research & Salvage Debris policy is active and a Salvager/Master Salvager is in the same system

salvaging.multiple_scientists: Salvager trait levels from all scientists in the system are summed. Master Salvager scientists multiply the resulting sum by 1.5.

research_output.base_production: Each research area has a base production of 10 research points per month, mostly from pops working researcher jobs or research stations on space deposits.
research_output.modifier_categories: Total empire production, job output, research stations / starbase buildings, researcher job category. Governor traits give full effect on their governed planet and half to sector planets.

research_speed.formula: Effective research production = Research resource produced × Σ Research speed modifiers
notable_sources: Expertise: Propulsion III councilor scientist: +15% (matching tech field)
No Head of Research councilor: −25% penalty
research_speed.other_sources: Many permanent, renewable, and temporary modifiers from events, anomalies, and traditions.

ethics.name: Ethics
ethics.description: Ethics represent an empire's or pop's core ideological beliefs and determine favored actions. Empires may have three moderate ethics, one fanatic plus one moderate, or be Gestalt. Pops have one moderate ethic each. Similar ethics improve relations; opposing ethics cause friction.

species_traits.name: Species Traits
species_traits.description: Traits represent a species' innate functions, abilities, and personality. They are set at species level but can be modified per pop via genetic modification. Traits affect pop output, faction attraction, and can be mutually exclusive. Some situations grant additional traits.

economy.name: Economy
economy.description: The economy is based on production and consumption of resources and services, primarily from pops working jobs, with secondary space deposits. Resources can be material (basic, advanced, strategic, rare) or abstract. Exceeding storage capacity loses excess production; shortages trigger penalties or situations. The market allows buying/selling for Trade, with prices affected by supply, demand, and market fee.

resources.name: Energy Credits
resources.description: Stored units of transferrable energy; used for ship/building upkeep, clearing blockers, and deals.

resources.name: Minerals
resources.description: Basic construction material; used for buildings, stations, and refining into alloys or consumer goods.

resources.name: Food
resources.description: Nutrients to sustain and grow pops; not used by Machine Intelligences.

resources.name: Trade Value
resources.description: Everyday economic activity; used for ship/colony upkeep and buying other resources.

resources.name: Biomass
resources.description: Raw organic potential (Wilderness origin only); used for buildings and terraforming.

resources.name: Alloys
resources.description: Advanced resource for military applications; used for ships, starbases, megastructures.

resources.name: Consumer Goods
resources.description: Gadgets and luxuries for pops; required by researcher and priest jobs. Not used by Hive Minds, generally not by Machine Intelligences.

resources.name: Exotic Gases
resources.description: Used for advanced weapon/shield components, edicts, and advanced buildings. Gathered from deposits or refined.

market.description: The market allows buying/selling resources for Trade. Prices range 20%-500% of base, subject to market fee (30% base, min 5%). Bulk and automated monthly trades use stored resources. The galactic market is founded via Galactic Community focus, with a lottery based on trade value and nomination rating.
market.galactic_market_mechanics: Nomination rating from Weak to Perfect, winning weight = (rating * system trade value)^1.5. Host gets -10% market fee.

strata.description: Jobs are divided into three strata for individualist empires (Elite, Specialist, Worker) and two for gestalt empires (Complex Drone, Menial Drone). Higher strata jobs fill first. Gestalt empires do not have an Elite stratum.

elite.description: Ruling class jobs that provide Amenities, Unity, and sometimes special bonuses. Provided by capital buildings. Examples: Politician, Science Director, High Priest, Merchant, Noble.
elite.source: Capital buildings (Planetary Administration up to Imperial Palace) and certain civics (Aristocratic Elite, Technocracy, Merchant Guilds, Exalted Priesthood).

specialist_complex_drone.description: Advanced jobs producing research, alloys, consumer goods, unity, trade, and strategic resources. Pops under Chattel Slavery cannot work these jobs. Some other slave types may work certain Specialist jobs.

worker_menial_drone.description: Basic resource jobs (Energy, Minerals, Food) and other low-stratum roles. Includes Colonists, Clerks, Maintenance Drones, Soldiers, and strategic resource gatherers from planetary deposits.
worker_menial_drone.special: Prison Industrial jobs are Worker-stratum versions of industrial jobs with lower output and upkeep, exclusive to Penal Colonies.

slave_jobs.description: Jobs only fillable by enslaved pops. Include Servants, Livestock, Toilers, Grid Amalgamated, Battle Thralls. Each tied to a specific slavery type.

resettlement.description: Instantly moves a pop between planets at a cost of 100 Energy and 10 Unity per 100 free pops, and 50 Energy per 100 enslaved pops. Resettling the last pop costs an additional 200 Influence and abandons the world. Requires Allowed Resettlement policy, Cosmogenesis ascension perk, or the pop to be a non‑sapient robot.

migration.description: Civilian pops that are not enslaved and have no migration controls automatically resettle monthly. Base rate: 10 pops/month across the empire. Requires a planet colonised for >60 months, with available jobs and housing, and habitability ≥20%.

modifiers.source: Council leader with Frontier Spirit I/II

migration.note: Pops can migrate to other empires if a Migration Treaty exists. Destination chosen by hidden weight based on stability and Automatic Resettlement Destination Chance modifiers.

happiness.description: Each pop has a happiness rating from 0% to 100%, base 50%. Happiness determines approval rating when weighted by political power.

above_50: +1% governing ethics attraction per point (free pops)
−0.02 crime per point (max 0 crime at 100% happiness)
below_50: −1% governing ethics attraction per point (free pops)

happiness.sources: Planetary edicts, civics, living standards, planet modifiers, ruler traits, faction approval, events.

modifiers.source: Under One Rule origin (Strengthened Government II/III)

political_power.stratum_specific_multipliers: Living standards and other sources provide different multipliers for Elite, Specialist, Worker, Civilian, and Slave strata (see in‑game tooltip or separate living‑standards table).

approval_rating.formula: Approval Rating = Σ (happiness × political power) / Σ (political power)
approval_rating.description: Weighted average of pop happiness by political power. Pops without happiness count as 50%. Rating is rounded down.

types: Base upkeep (resource depends on species trait, can be modified; cannot go below −90%)
Living standards upkeep: Consumer goods based on stratum and living standards (non‑Gestalt pops, including Bio‑Trophies, and synthetics with citizen rights).
Job upkeep: additional resources required for specific jobs
Housing: each pop requires 1 housing by default (can be modified)
Amenities: base requirements are 1 amenities per free pop, 0.75 per slave, 0.5 per non‑citizen robot. Habitability below 100% increases base upkeep and amenity needs.

housing.description: Living space for pops. Each pop requires 1 unit of housing by default, but this can be modified by species traits, living standards, and other factors. Housing is primarily provided by districts, especially City Districts.

effects.missing_housing: Each unit of missing housing causes −40 × (missing / total housing required) stability on the planet and a flat +50 planet emigration push.

housing.sources: Districts (City District, etc.), buildings (Luxury Residences, etc.), traditions/civics (Agrarian Idyll gives extra housing to resource districts).
housing.notes: Resource districts typically provide enough housing for their own jobs. Pops working building jobs need additional housing from City Districts or housing buildings. The capital building provides a small starting buffer.

amenities.description: Infrastructure and jobs fulfilling pop needs. High amenities grant happiness to free pops and stability to Gestalt Consciousness empires; low amenities lower happiness/stability.
amenities.inefficiency_penalty: 5 amenities are always lost on every colony.

formula.available: Available Amenities = (Amenities from Buildings + (Amenities from Jobs × Σ job-scope modifiers)) × Σ planet/empire-scope modifiers − (5 + Pop base Amenities Upkeep × Σ upkeep modifiers)
formula.rounding: Amenities from pop jobs are rounded down; all other amenity values are rounded to the nearest integer.
formula.happiness_bonus: min(20%, 20 × ⌊available⌋ / ⌊pop amenities usage⌋)  → scales from +0% at 0 available to +20% when available ≥ 2× pop usage.
formula.happiness_penalty: max(−50%, (200/3) × ⌊available⌋ / ⌊pop amenities usage⌋)  → scales from 0% at 0 available to −50% when available ≤ −75% of pop usage.

empire_wide_amenities_bonus.source: Vultaum Star Assembly secrets special project

slavery.description: Species can be enslaved by setting citizenship to Slaves or Servitude. Enslaved pops use 0.75 amenities and 0.75 housing per 100 pops, have resettlement cost of 50 Energy, cannot join factions, and have 75% reduced political power (further reduced by Slave Processing Facility). Low stability is more dangerous with slaves present: dangerous events begin below 40 stability instead of 25.

robots.description: Artificial Mechanical pops built after researching robotics technologies. They have climate preference traits for broad habitability. Their available jobs depend on AI policy and robot tech tier.

tiers.Artificial Specialists: Can fill Worker and Specialist jobs
tiers.Artificial Administration: Can fill all jobs (Elite if Citizen Rights)

robots.sapience: Researching Artificial Administration makes all robots sapient if AI policy is Servitude or Citizen Rights.

special_rules.Hive Mind: Cannot build robots; always purge captured robots.
special_rules.Machine pops: Always sapient; consume consumer goods and full housing/amenities regardless of AI policy.

purges.description: Purging is done by setting citizenship to Undesirables. Gestalt Consciousness pops in non‑Gestalt empires are automatically set to Undesirables (unless assimilated or Disconnected Drones policy is Sustain). Purge types determine decline rate, resource output, happiness penalty, and refugee chance.

types_summary.requirements: Individualist, not Hive‑Minded, no non‑sapient robots
not Hive‑Minded, not Machine Intelligence unless exception
War enemy founder species (Devouring War casus belli)

purges.opinion_penalties: Purged Our Species: −0.25 per pop (max −1000) for founder species; Genocidal: −0.5 to −0.15 depending on ethics, max −1000.

refugees.generation: Pops flee from purges, slavery, resettlement, crisis bombardment, Land Appropriation policy, or Expel Excess Population.
refugees.acceptance: Refugees go to empires that accept them via Refugees policy. 65% chance to empire with same founder species and a planet with ≥70% habitability and 100 housing; 35% chance to other empires based on desirability tiers.
refugees.bonuses: Accepted refugee pops get +20 happiness for 10 years and provide +10 intel on their origin empire.
refugees.restrictions: Hive‑Minded, Nerve Stapled, Zombie, and Suppressed pops cannot become refugees.

pop_growth.cap: Total growth across all species capped at 5.0 per month.
pop_growth.xenocompatibility: With Xeno‑Compatibility, all species combine for penalty calculation; growth distributed proportionally to species size.
pop_growth.probabilistic: Growth < 1 is probabilistic (e.g., 1.2 = 1 pop + 20% chance of second).

demographics.pop_assembly: Biological assembly (cloning) and mechanical assembly occur additionally; further details to be added.
demographics.pop_decline: Pops decline due to purging, overcrowding, Population Controls policy, or traits (Pathogenic Genes, Clone Soldier without Ancient Clone Vat).

jobs_system.description: A Job is where resources are produced on a planet. Most Jobs are created by Districts and Buildings and thus limited in number. Each Job can have its maximum number of pops reduced by a slider. Jobs are divided into strata; higher strata are filled before lower ones.

empire_variants.standard: Jobs are worked by pops. Output receives bonuses/penalties based on pop traits. Pops automatically fill jobs they are capable of, preferring higher strata jobs that best fit their traits based on a weighting system. Job priority can be manually adjusted. A pop that is more suitable can take a job from the current holder.
empire_variants.wilderness_origin: Jobs are worked without pops. Output receives bonuses/penalties based on primary species traits. The slider reduces resource consumption and production instead of max pops.

job_swaps.description: Some jobs change to alternative versions when conditions are met. The swapped job provides additional or different resources but inherits the same modifiers as the base job. If multiple swaps are possible, the one with the highest weight takes precedence.

examples.politician_to_noble: Aristocratic Elite civic → Noble, adds +0.5 Stability per 100 pops, weight 10
examples.politician_to_executive: Corporate authority → Executive, adds +4 Trade per 100 pops, weight 5
examples.science_director_technocracy: Technocracy civic or planet has Research Institute → Science Director, shifts Unity to research, weight 5
examples.merchant_guilds: Merchant Guilds civic or Galactic Stock Exchange → Merchant, shifts Unity to Trade, weight 10
examples.high_priest_exalted: Exalted Priesthood civic or Citadel of Faith → High Priest, +1 Unity, weight 10

job_categories.description: Most jobs belong to a category that groups similar jobs together. This allows modifiers (buildings, technologies, etc.) to apply to all jobs in that category. Sub-categories inherit multiplicative modifiers from the parent category; only some sub-categories also inherit additive modifiers.

cross_category_inheritance.description: A few jobs inherit modifiers from categories they don't formally belong to, e.g., Lord Commander/Knight inherit from Soldiers; Livestock inherits from Farmers (if non-Lithoid) or Miners (if Lithoid); Grid Amalgamated inherits from Technicians.

ascension_jobs.description: Specialist jobs unlocked by ascension paths.
ascension_jobs.psionic: Telepath (Psi Corps): +9 Unity, -10 Crime, +5% Resources from Psionic pops (up to +15% with Shroud breach and Divine Conduit councilor)
ascension_jobs.cybernetic: Augmentor (Augmentation Center): +6 Engineering (+3 if Cybernetic main species), +10% Pop growth speed (or +5% for Hive), plus Trade bonuses with Augmentation Bazaars
ascension_jobs.synthetic: Identity Designer (Identity Complex): +5 Engineering (+2 if Mechanical main species), +1 Mechanical pop assembly empire-wide

special_jobs.assimilation: Pops set to Assimilation citizenship enter Assimilation stratum and 'Undergoing Assimilation' job; effects depend on assimilation type.
special_jobs.purging: Pops set to Undesirables enter Undesirables stratum and 'Purging' job; resource output varies by purge type (e.g., Forced Labor: +3 Food +3 Minerals per 100 pops). Fanatic Purifiers/Devouring Swarm gain additional Unity/Society research per purged pop.
special_jobs.subversive: High crime/deviancy adds Criminal, Deviant Drone, or Corrupt Drone jobs with negative trade/energy output. Pops abandon them if better jobs become available.
special_jobs.pre-sapients: Pre-sapient pops use a special stratum; policy determines job: default 'Pre-Sapient' (idle), 'Hunted' (Livestock output), 'Protected' (Amenities with some food/minerals consumption).
special_jobs.bio-trophy: In Rogue Servitor empires, organic pops become Bio-Trophies (+2 Unity, +1% Complex Drone output, housing needs covered).
special_jobs.neural_chip: Pops on a Synaptic Lathe become Computing Components; job provides scaling research based on total Neural Chips / 20 and Advanced Logic.

origin_jobs.broken_shackles: Starts with low-tech jobs (Administrator, Supervisor, Preacher, Scientist, Militia, Laborer, Prospector, Reactor Engineer, Homesteader, Scavenger) that upgrade to normal jobs later.
origin_jobs.knights_of_the_toxic_god: Lord Commander (Elite), Knight (Specialist), Squire (Worker) on home habitat and consecrated habitats. Outputs scale with Quest choices. Squires boost Knight/Lord Commander output by +2% each.
origin_jobs.primal_calling: Wrangler jobs with variants per stance (Wrangler, Rancher, Trophy Hunter, Animal Handler) and gestalt equivalents (Wrangler Drone, Symbiosis Integrator, Hunter Drone). Warrior Culture civic gives Beastfighter.

overlord_jobs.description: Jobs created by Overlord holdings on subject planets. Upkeep paid by the subject. Subject cannot change priority. Provide resources to overlord and sometimes boost subject.

examples.overlord_trader: Emporium: +7 Energy to overlord, +10 Amenities, -7 Energy for subject
examples.overlord_academic: Satellite Campus: +3 Research to overlord, +1 Research, upkeep depends on subject type
examples.overlord_propagandist: Ministry of Truth: +0.15 Influence to overlord, -1 Unity for subject
examples.overlord_metallurgist: Offworld Foundry: +3 Alloys to overlord, -6 Minerals for subject

event_jobs.description: Jobs from planetary features or planet modifiers, often giving specialized output.
notable: Subterranean Liaison Officer: +6 Trade/Energy, -1 housing usage
Odd Factory Worker: +4 Alloys, no upkeep (Worker stratum)
Dimensional Portal Researcher: +12 Physics, upkeep varies
Space-Time Anomaly Researcher: +8 Physics +4 Engineering, +0.2 Dark Matter
Munitions Decommissioner: +10 Engineering, +1 Alloys, +1 Minor artifacts
Gas Plant Engineer: +3 Exotic Gases, +5% Exotic Gases from Jobs

pre_ftl_jobs.description: Pre-FTL civilizations use normal strata but jobs provide negligible output. Standard empires and Hive Minds have their own sets for pre-industrial and industrial ages. Unique pre-FTLs (e.g., Ketlings, Habinte) have special jobs. Fallen Empires use a Precursor stratum with unique jobs like Archivist, Xeno-Keeper, and 'Acolyte of the...' jobs.

war.description: War is a near‑inevitable aspect of the game as long as at least one non‑Fanatic Pacifist empire exists. Wars determine mid‑ to late‑game borders. Each side states a wargoal, and resolution depends on war exhaustion, occupation, and fleet strength.

common_effects.status_quo: Each side keeps all systems they have fully occupied if they have claims on them, except the defender's capital (which stays with the defender).
common_effects.victory: Winner gains control of all systems with claims from the loser, even if not occupied. All systems occupied by the loser are returned, regardless of claims.
common_effects.surrender_acceptance_modifier: −10 per unoccupied claimed system, −100 per unoccupied claimed colonized system.

war.war_participation: Multiple empires can be on either side; the declarer and the declared‑upon are the war leaders. Only war leaders can offer/accept surrender or status quo. Federation war‑declaration rules depend on the War Declaration law; subjects may or may not join based on Overlord Conflicts terms.
war.ai_war_warning: AI empires moving fleets to a border before declaring war; targeted empires with military intel receive a warning (more precise with higher intel).

casus_belli_and_wargoals.description: A casus belli is required to start a war; each grants at least one wargoal type. After declaration, the defender has one year to choose its own wargoals.

counterattack.requirements: Enemy of My Enemy resolution (3), target not in Galactic Community, target in offensive war against a GC member

humiliate.victory_effect: Gain 100 Influence; loser loses all Influence, −33% monthly influence gain, −10% happiness for 10 years. If loser had Supremacist stance, changed to Belligerent.
humiliate.status_quo_effect: No extra effect beyond common effects

impose_ideology.requirements: War Philosophy policy set to Liberation Wars; target is NOT Gestalt Consciousness
impose_ideology.victory_effect: Loser and all its individualist subjects shift ethics towards victor, rulers replaced. Loser leaves federation if in one, rivalries with attacker ended. Authority shifts to victor's unless victor is Corporate. Shared Burdens/Crusader Spirit civics applied or removed accordingly. Sovereign civic removed if present.
impose_ideology.status_quo_effect: If at least one colonized system is fully occupied by attacker, all fully occupied systems are turned into a new empire with attacker's ethics and Liberated origin.

despoliation.victory_effect: Take 250 Energy and 250 Minerals from the loser for every planet they control (even if they lack the resources). Loser gets −20% Energy and Minerals for 10 years. On capturing the capital during war, 90% chance to steal one of the defender's relics.
despoliation.status_quo_effect: No resource transfer, only common effects

expropriation.requirements: No Commercial Pact with target; not in the same Federation; corporate empire has at least one branch office on an owned planet
expropriation.victory_effect: Corporate empire closes all branch offices on attacker's planets. Attacker gains 1000 Trade per branch office.
expropriation.status_quo_effect: Corporate empire closes all branch offices on attacker's planets; corporate gains 1000 Trade per branch office.

hostile_takeover.requirements: Both empires have Corporate authority; target has at least one branch office; NOT Criminal Heritage civic
hostile_takeover.victory_effect: All branch offices on planets whose owners have a commercial pact or federation with the victor are transferred to the victor. If loser is in a federation, branch offices on other members' planets are kept.
hostile_takeover.status_quo_effect: Same as victory, but only for fully occupied systems

claim_the_galatron.requirements: Target empire used the Galatron relic; War Philosophy set to Unrestricted Wars; target is NOT Gestalt Consciousness
claim_the_galatron.victory_effect: Gain the Galatron relic and 100 Influence. Loser loses all Influence, −33% monthly influence gain, −10% happiness for 10 years.

galatron_access_denied.requirements: Target used Galatron relic; target has closed borders; NOT Driven Assimilator or Genocidal empire
galatron_access_denied.victory_effect: Gain Galatron relic and 100 Influence. Loser loses all Influence, −33% monthly influence gain, −10% happiness for 10 years.

trial_of_advancement.requirements: Target is member of the Imperial Council; By Trial of Advancement resolution
trial_of_advancement.victory_effect: Replace the loser in the Imperial Council

pirate_raid.requirements: Letters of Marque civic or Captain Ness
pirate_raid.victory_effect: Take 500 Energy and 50 Minerals from loser per planet (even if insufficient). Loser gets −20% Energy and Minerals for 10 years.

proxy_war_cb.requirements: Can only be used for a Proxy War

punishment.requirements: Fallen empire's demand refused or rivalry with fallen empire
punishment.victory_effect: Refused demands enforced; if non‑Gestalt, ruler killed (unless Ancient Caretakers). Loser loses all Influence, −33% monthly influence, −10% happiness for 10 years. Supremacist stance becomes Belligerent. If Cosmogenesis ascension perk, also −50% research speed for 10 years.

subjugation_cbs.description: Subjugation CBs seek to end the independence of the target and any subjects it has. Surrender acceptance is fixed at −100. Cannot target fallen empires, the Custodian, or the Galactic Emperor. Proxy War cost: 200 Influence.
subjugation_cbs.status_quo_special: If at least one colonized system is fully occupied, all fully occupied systems become a new empire with Liberated origin and the victory conditions imposed on it.

vassalize.requirements: Superior relative power; NOT Driven Assimilator, Devouring Swarm, Fanatic Purifiers (unless same species), Determined Exterminator (unless machine)

establish_hegemony.requirements: President of level 3+ Hegemony federation; target not in a federation; target becomes Hegemony member

bring_into_fold_scion.requirements: Scion origin; target becomes Scion of same overlord

bring_into_fold_nemesis.requirements: Galactic Nemesis ascension perk; NOT Genocidal

imperial_crusade.requirements: Imperial Crusade resolution; target becomes Imperial member

paired_cbs.description: Pairs of CBs that force the defender to use the counterpart if the attacker uses one.

pairs.notes: Subject vs overlord; subject not in federation with overlord
President refused member leaving federation; federation not at war
Secret Fealty from target's subject; all pledging subjects join attacker
From Spark Rebellion operation; victory restores GC, loss gives Imperium authority
Rivalry with Emperor; victory gives 100 Influence, imposes happiness/influence penalties, stance change

total_war_cbs.description: Total War CBs cause automatic ownership change on occupation (system goes to the participant with the highest claim count, or nearest participant if none). Surrender is impossible – war only ends with total conquest or status quo. Defeated empires lose unclaimed starbases, territory becomes neutral. Proxy War with Total War CB costs 200 Influence.

list.name: Devastation
list.requirements: Scorched World Heralds or Pyromanic Instinct

list.name: Absorption
list.requirements: Devouring Swarm or Behemoth Fury crisis level 5

list.name: Wipe Them Out
list.requirements: Existential Campaigns tech or Galactic Nemesis ascension perk
list.special: Starbases instantly destroyed instead of captured

list.name: Psionic Containment
list.requirements: Mindwardens against Psionic or Latent Psionic founder species

list.name: Colossus / Stop Colossus
list.requirements: Either empire has a Colossus; war not ended if colossus destroyed

list.name: Preemptive War / End Threat
list.requirements: Mutual Defense resolution 5; one empire not in GC

list.name: Crisis War
list.requirements: Declare Crisis resolution; can be ended by Repeal Crisis resolution

list.name: Machine Uprising
list.requirements: Creator empire and AI rebellion; victor gains 2000-6000 Unity and 200-600 Influence

list.name: First Contact Worst Contact
list.requirements: Fear of the Dark first contact choice

claims.description: Claims represent territorial ambitions and determine which systems a victor will control. Base cost: 50 Influence.
cost_modifiers: +25 per hyperlane distance from owned territory
+25 if system has an upgraded starbase (not a basic outpost)
+100% total cost during an offensive war
−20% if claimed system belongs to a Rival
Total cost can be reduced to 0 if modifiers reach −100%
rules: Unrestricted Warfare policy allows claiming any system not owned by a Federation member.
Defenders in a defensive war can freely claim attacker systems regardless of War Philosophy.
Losing a system gives the former owner 10 claims on it.
Claims cannot be rescinded during a war.
Winning any war enforces all claims before applying wargoal effects — if annexing all claims would destroy the target, CBs like Ideology or Subjugation become unavailable.

war_exhaustion.description: War Exhaustion ranges from 0% to 100%. It accumulates from space/ground combat losses, planetary destruction (Colossus or Armageddon bombardment), and passive attrition over time. At 100%, the opposing side can enforce a Status Quo after 24 months. If both sides reach 100%, Status Quo is enforced automatically after 24 months.
war_exhaustion.surrender_auto: If an empire and all its allies lose control of all systems and planets during a war, it will automatically surrender.
war_exhaustion.reduction: War exhaustion gain can be reduced by ethics, traditions, technology, and claim count. If total modifiers reach −100%, no war exhaustion is accumulated.

proxy_war.description: Empires with the Secret Societies civic can start wars between other empires, provided all empires have completed First Contact with each other. Requires either 70 Trust or 60 Infiltration with the empire that will declare the Proxy War. Multiple empires can be invited to the attacker side. Excluded for Fanatic Xenophobe, Barbaric Despoilers, Criminal Heritage, Driven Assimilator, Bodysnatcher, or Genocidal empires.
proxy_war.discovery: 10% chance per invited attacker that the instigation is discovered. If discovered, every empire on either side gets −200 opinion with the instigator, and if in the Galactic Community, the instigator receives −20% Diplomatic Weight for 10 years.
proxy_war.subterfuge_tradition: Empires with the Subterfuge tradition tree also gain proxy war ability but at +25% cost.

ships.description: Spaceborne vessels controlled by an empire. Primary way of interacting with objects and entities. Classified into civilian (individually controlled) and military (form fleets). All ships are built at starbases or orbital rings with shipyard modules, or at a Mega Shipyard or Juggernaut. Each shipyard module builds/upgrades one ship at a time. Ships can be repaired at any owned, operational starbase above outpost level.

shipset.description: Each empire uses a shipset that determines visual appearance. Random empires match shipset to phenotype. With BioGenesis DLC, shipsets are categorized as Mechanical or Biological, which affects construction/upkeep resources, available hull sizes, weapon types, and tech tree branches.

biological.resources: Alloys and Food for construction and upkeep
biological.growth_stages: three growth stages (other designs have single stage)

civilian_ships.description: Unarmed vessels controlled individually. Shields, armor, and core components auto-upgrade on tech unlock at no cost; do not need to return to starbase. Monthly upkeep: 1 energy per ship (except transports). Default evasive stance; can be set to passive. Construction and Science ships cost: 100 alloys (mechanical) or 175 food + 50 alloys (biological).

abilities: Build outposts, mining stations, research stations, observation posts, and megastructure sites
Auto-build mining and research stations via fleet order

mechanical_cost.influence: 75 per hyperlane from closest owned system
mechanical_cost.alloys: 100 if not Machine Intelligence, 150 if Machine Intelligence

biological_cost.influence: 75 per hyperlane from closest owned system
biological_cost.alloys: 50 if not Machine Intelligence, 75 if Machine Intelligence
biological_cost.food: 175 if not Machine Intelligence, 263 if Machine Intelligence

outpost.description: Starbase used to claim systems and expand borders. Only built in orbit around a star. Full cost depends on target system.

mining_station.description: Collects Minerals, Energy Credits, and Strategic Resources from uninhabited planets, stars, asteroids. Energy-producing mining stations have no upkeep.

research_station.description: Collects Physics, Society, and Engineering research from uninhabited planets, stars, asteroids.

observation_post.description: Built in orbit around planets with primitive civilizations to study their society.

megastructure_site.cost: Alloys, Unity, Influence depending on megastructure
megastructure_site.biological_cost: Food, Alloys, Unity, Influence depending on megastructure

construction_ship.tier3_bulwark_subject: Can construct Battlewright construction ships, giving all friendly ships in the system +5% Daily Hull Regen (requires Overlord DLC).

science_ship.description: Primary exploration vessel; requires a scientist to operate. Conducts surveys to discover resources and anomalies, investigates debris for research/tech unlocks, excavates archaeological sites, explores astral rifts. Science ships will always equip the most advanced cloaking device researched (with First Contact DLC).
science_ship.tier3_scholarium_subject: Can construct Arctrellis science ships, giving enemy ships with AI combat computers −25% Accuracy, Fire Rate, and Ship Speed in the same system (requires Overlord DLC).

colony_ship.description: Settles habitable planets or megastructures in owned systems. Selecting a species/subspecies sends that pop (pops are not consumed/transported). One year build time; upkeep maintained throughout colonization. Species must have Colonization Allowed rights. Can be built via world interface. Cost depends on empire and founder species class (see detailed cost tables). Private Colony Ships cost 500 trade (Corporate Dominion/Private Prospectors civics). Lithoid Meteorites cost 500 minerals, no upkeep, 150 days build, faster sublight, and add two Buried Lithoids blockers and a Lithoid Crater modifier (Calamitous Birth origin). Genesis Arks replace normal colony ships for Genesis Guides civics, granting a species with 300 Pre-Sapient pops and a Genesis Preserve feature. Synthetic Fertility origin uses cost as Mechanical empires. Wilderness origin cannot build colony ships; colonize by terraforming. Councilors with Frontier Spirit trait reduce colony ship cost by −10%.

transport_ship.description: Carries assault armies. Armies embarking generate a transport ship automatically. No additional energy upkeep despite army upkeep. Form separate transport fleets (listed in fleet manager). Unarmed; must be escorted. Default passive stance; can be set to aggressive to follow a military fleet and auto-land on enemy planets with similar or less army strength. Required for some special projects. Will always equip the most advanced cloaking device researched (with First Contact DLC).

military_ships.description: Armed vessels. Require Energy and Alloys as monthly maintenance; trade upkeep if not stationed at a starbase. Derive most stats from components; customizable via ship designer. Classified by hull size: Corvette, Frigate, Destroyer, Cruiser, Battleship, and Titan (Apocalypse DLC). Progenitor Hive origin can build Offspring versions of Corvettes, Destroyers, Cruisers, Battleships. Galactic Nemesis ascension perk unlocks Menacing Corvettes, Destroyers, Cruisers. Titans built only at Citadel with Titan Assembly Yards or Mega Shipyard. Regular ships built at any starbase/orbital ring with shipyard module, or Mega Shipyard/Juggernaut.

techs.standardized_patterns: Reduces build cost by −5%, increases build speed by +25% for each class except Titan.
techs.hull_improvements: Improved <size> Hulls (+10% hull points each), then Advanced <size> Hulls (+10% hull points each). Corvettes and Frigates share these techs.

special_hulls.fallen_empires: Battlecruisers and Escorts (direct upgrades to Battleships/Destroyers).

fleets.description: Group of military or transport ships operating as a single unit. Can be assigned a Commander as admiral (military) or general (transport). Military fleets maintain a fleet template via fleet manager; can have a home base. All ships in a fleet stay in the same system, synchronize FTL. Merging can be manual or auto-reinforcement. Reinforcing builds missing ships at nearest available starbase, ships enter transit mode to meet fleet. Manually built military ships auto-merge with fleets in orbit at same starbase; new fleet created if no capacity.

upgrading_ships.description: When design updated, ships must be upgraded manually at a starbase/orbital ring with shipyard module, Mega Shipyard, or Juggernaut. Upgrade cost = difference in construction cost between new and current design. If new design costs less, upgrade free but no refund. Multiple ships upgrade simultaneously if multiple shipyards. Ships upgrade to same design name; if not found, most-recently saved design of same class. Event ships with unresearched components should not be upgraded (replaces with latest researched, possibly inferior).

naval_capacity.description: Represents number of military ships empire can effectively support. Exceeding it increases ship maintenance by % equal to the % exceeded (e.g., 50% over = +50% upkeep). Base naval capacity: 20. Maximum 9999, but can still build over if economy supports upkeep.
naval_capacity.titan_limit: 1 base; +1 per 200 naval capacity, max 20 at 3800 naval capacity.

naval_coverage.power_projection: Adds monthly influence based on ratio of naval coverage to empire size. If coverage ≥ empire size: +2 monthly influence (Imperial authority: +3). If less, scaled by percentage ratio (e.g., 50% coverage gives +1 monthly influence, Imperial +1.5).

fleet_command_limit.description: Determines how large one individual fleet can be. Base command limit: 50, increased by various sources.

ship_rank.description: Ships gain experience by being in combat: +5 exp per day in combat.

colossal_ships.description: Largest ships: Colossus, Juggernaut, Star-Eater. Require Citadel with Colossal Assembly Yards or Mega Shipyard. Each can only be built once (except Star-Eater), but can be rebuilt if lost. Integrating a subject with a colossal ship grants it to the overlord (bypasses limit), but cannot be rebuilt if lost. Colossal ships cannot use Phase Fleet order.

colossus.description: Single-purpose weapons platform built around a Planet-Killer weapon slot. Lacks weapons to attack fleets; ignores enemies to maneuver straight to target (unless stance set to Evasive). Fallen Empires have 10% chance to start with one and can build more if awakened. Requires Colossi technology from Colossus Project ascension perk special project. Halfway through, choose starting weapon; can later research others and retrofit. Owning a colossus grants Colossus casus belli on all and gives others Stop Colossus casus belli. Using a colossus causes large amounts of war exhaustion on target empire and +50% ethics attraction toward Militarist and Xenophobe. Against pre-FTL: requires system ownership and Aggressive Interference Native Interference policy; gives −40 opinion with non-Xenophobe/non-Xenophile/non-Genocidal empires and −60 with Xenophiles.

weapons.name: Global Pacifier
weapons.effect: Target becomes Shielded variant, gains deposit of 10 Society Research.

opinion_modifiers.specific_ethics: −20 (Egalitarian, Fanatic Egalitarian, Xenophile, Fanatic Xenophile, Militarist, Fanatic Militarist)

weapons.special: If used on a Holy World, Holy Guardians instantly awaken and declare war.

weapons.name: World Cracker
weapons.effect: Habitable planet becomes Shattered World, habitable megastructure ruined. Habitable planet gains deposit of 4–16 Minerals.

opinion_modifiers.specific_ethics: −100 (Egalitarian, Fanatic Egalitarian, Xenophile, Fanatic Xenophile, Pacifist, Fanatic Pacifist)

weapons.special: Fanatic Purifiers/Determined Exterminator gain 600–3000 Unity on first use. If Earth shattered, 50% chance of creating wormhole to another system. Can be test fired on uninhabited planets. On Holy World triggers Holy Guardians awakening.

weapons.name: Neutron Sweep
weapons.effect: All pops killed; planet gets −30% Habitability for 10 years.

opinion_modifiers.specific_ethics: −80 (Egalitarian, Fanatic Egalitarian, Xenophile, Fanatic Xenophile, Spiritualist, Fanatic Spiritualist, Pacifist, Fanatic Pacifist)

weapons.requirements: Not Teachers of the Shroud origin. If Spiritualist: Telepathy tech (without Utopia) OR Mind Over Matter OR Interdimensional Processing.
weapons.special: Fanatic Purifiers/Determined Exterminator gain 600–3000 Unity on first use. Infested Worlds can be targeted and turned into Barren Worlds with Terraforming Candidate modifier.

weapons.name: Divine Enforcer
weapons.effect: Pops change ethics to Spiritualist; pops without ethic killed. World gets +999% Spiritualist ethic attraction for 10 years. Owned worlds get −20% happiness for 10 years.

opinion_modifiers.specific_ethics: −40 (Materialist, Fanatic Materialist, Egalitarian, Fanatic Egalitarian, Machine Intelligence, Spiritualist, Fanatic Spiritualist)

weapons.requirements: Spiritualist. Any of: Telepathy tech (without Utopia), Mind Over Matter, Interdimensional Processing, Teachers of the Shroud origin. AI Worlds can be targeted and turned into Broken Worlds.

weapons.name: Nanobot Diffuser
weapons.effect: Biological pops assimilated and gain Cybernetic trait. Mechanical, Machine, Hive-Minded pops killed. Can target owned worlds if any pop assimilable. Gain ownership if not owned.

weapons.name: Devolving Beam
weapons.effect: Biological armies destroyed. Most biological pops become pre-sapient with Forcefully Devolved trait; 1 biological pop unaffected. With Archaeo-Engineers ascension perk: Mechanical and Machine pops killed, robot armies destroyed.

opinion_modifiers.specific_ethics: −80 (Spiritualist, Fanatic Spiritualist, Xenophile, Fanatic Xenophile, Hive Mind)

weapons.requirements: Ancient Relics DLC. Must wait at least 1 year before targeting same world again. If perpetrator has Archaeo-Engineers: AI Worlds can be targeted and turned into Broken Worlds.

weapons.name: Planet Smelter
weapons.effect: Pops not Thermophile, Lithoid, Machine, or Mechanical killed. Habitable planet becomes Volcanic World; uninhabitable becomes Molten World. Can target owned planet. If pops remain, gain ownership.
weapons.requirements: Thermophile founder species OR Red Giant OR Galactic Hyperthermia OR Planet Forgers (Planetary Architects). Ring Worlds, Habitats, Volcanic Worlds cannot be targeted. Can test fire on uninhabited planets. On Holy World triggers Holy Guardians awakening.

weapons.name: Deluge Machine
weapons.effect: Pops without Aquatic traits killed. All districts removed. Planet terraformed into Ocean World. Can target owned planet. If pops remain, gain ownership.
weapons.requirements: Hydrocentric ascension perk. AI Worlds can be targeted and turned into Broken Worlds. Ring Worlds, Habitats, Ocean Worlds cannot be targeted. On Holy World triggers Holy Guardians awakening.

unique_colossi.name: Toxic Entity
unique_colossi.source: Knights of the Toxic God origin story
unique_colossi.effect: Turns habitable planets into Toxic Worlds with Toxic Terraforming Candidate modifier. Same opinion penalties as World Cracker.

unique_colossi.name: Cetana's Colossus
unique_colossi.source: Defeating Synthetic Queen crisis via Shroud option
unique_colossi.effect: Uses World Cracker; largest component loadout in game.

juggernaut.description: Mobile center of operations. Has 10× Titan hull points, 6 Hangar slots, 2 turreted XL slots, unique aura components (stronger than Titan auras). No logistical upkeep; reduces logistical upkeep of ships in same system by 75%. Has 6 shipyards (can build/upgrade 6 ships at a time); cannot build Titans/colossal ships but can upgrade/repair them. Can upgrade itself or at any shipyard. Auto-repairs after battle like stations. Can retreat and go MIA. Fleets can set Juggernaut as homebase (upgrade/repair orders update in real time only when stationary). Galactic Defense Force/Imperial Armada can have their own (allowing Custodian/Emperor to have two). Fallen Empires have no cap and cannot build them.

star_eater.description: Massive superweapon used by Crisis Aspirant at level 5 Galactic Nemesis after declaring war as Existential Threat. Main weapon targets a star's core, collapsing it into a black hole and destroying the system, collecting dark matter to fuel Aetherophasic Engine. Final crisis level grants two Star-Eaters and tech to build more. Equipped with regular weapons (can fight in fleet combat). Building cost uses unchanging dark matter amount. Star cracking destroys all fleets in system except firing Star-Eater; they go MIA. Multi-star systems: cracking any star destroys all. Grants dark matter based on galaxy size: Tiny 3000, Small 2500, Medium 2000, Large 1500, Huge 1000. Can target own stars unless system contains empire capital. On Holy World triggers Holy Guardians awakening. Star-Eaters cannot use Phase Fleet order like other colossal ships.

cloaking.description: Allows a fleet to become invisible, ignore closed borders, not go MIA if war declared. Science ships can conduct Active Reconnaissance. Cloaking components disable shields (some exceptions with restrictions). Can only cloak every 120 days; automatically decloak if attacking. Cloaked ships marked with horizontal stripes on galaxy map. Science and transport ships always equipped with latest cloaking device. Each ship has Cloaking Strength; fleet strength = lowest in fleet. Determines penalties and detection threshold.

strength_levels.very_low_1: −50% Sublight Speed, +125% Hyper Jump Charge Time.
strength_levels.low_2: −40% Sublight Speed, +100% Hyper Jump Charge Time.
strength_levels.medium_3: −30% Sublight Speed, +75% Hyper Jump Charge Time.
strength_levels.high_4: −20% Sublight Speed, +50% Hyper Jump Charge Time.
strength_levels.very_high_5: −10% Sublight Speed, +25% Hyper Jump Charge Time.

cloaking.detection: Cloaked fleet detected if within sensor range of starbase with Detection Strength ≥ fleet's Cloaking Strength, or empire has max Intel on cloaked fleet owner, or sensor link with detecting empire. Non-allied detected fleets forced to decloak in border.

sources: +1–5 from cloaking Auxiliary components (size-dependent)
+1 from Subterfuge tradition tree
+1 from Criminal Heritage civic
+1 from Obsidian Veil edict (Dark Consortium/Shadow Corporation)
+1 from Astral Cloaking edict
+1 from Trickster admiral trait
+1 from Material Liberator II commander trait
+2 from Guerilla Tactician commander destiny trait
+2 from Enigmatic Engineering ascension perk
+2 from covenant with Whisperers in the Void
+2 from Confidential Research I scientist trait
+3 from Confidential Research II scientist trait
+3 from Confidential Research III scientist trait
+3 if fleet inside a Nebula
+10 if fleet inside a Stardust Storm

holdings.description: A holding represents an area on a world owned by an empire other than the world's owner. There are two types: overlord holdings and branch offices. Both provide vision in the system. Only one of each holding per world. Construction speed is modified by the system owner's bonuses/penalties, not the holder's. Destroys if subject becomes independent (overlord holdings) or can be closed at will (branch offices).
holdings.sources: Overlord DLC, MegaCorp DLC, other DLCs for specific holdings

overlord_holdings.description: Constructed by overlords on their subjects' worlds. Total number per subject limited by Holdings Limit in the subject agreement, modified by Bureaucratic Surveillance resolutions. Can be spread across planets or concentrated on one. Default to subject's capital if built from the Subject Agreement menu. Destroyed if subject becomes independent.
overlord_holdings.effects: Each holding provides jobs to the world, transfers resources, adds modifiers, and usually affects monthly loyalty (reducing it). Detailed stats and effects are in the buildings data section.
overlord_holdings.sources: Overlord DLC, DLCs for origin/civic holdings

branch_offices.description: Constructed by empires with Corporate authority. Gives corporate empire Trade equal to 50% of world's Trade. Only one branch office per world, by any empire. Cannot be built on Nanite Worlds or in systems owned by Gestalt Consciousness, Fanatic Purifiers, or fallen empires. Base cost: 1000 Trade and 50 Influence, increasing 10% per hyperlane jump beyond 3, plus 5 empire size. Up to four corporate building slots unlocked by world capital tier. Buildings add jobs and bonuses to the planet, scaling with civics and Trade. Closed by owner at any time; if relationship is lost, owner gains Expropriation CB but office remains.

criminal_heritage.description: Criminal Heritage empires can create 'illegal' branch offices without a commercial pact. Trade output ranges from 25% to 75% of world's Trade depending on Crime. Illegal buildings create Crime and Criminal jobs. Legal/illegal buildings convert based on relationship. World owner gains Expropriation CB; 5% monthly chance to close if Crime reaches 0, with 10-year cooldown before reestablishment. At war/truce with system owner prevents establishment.

branch_offices.effects: Corporate buildings provide Trade, resources, and jobs. Illegal variants produce Crime and Criminal jobs. Many scale with civics that add extra Trader jobs on the corporation's capital. Detailed stats in buildings data.
branch_offices.sources: MegaCorp DLC, The Machine Age DLC (advanced authority), other DLCs for specific buildings

stability.description: A measure of the overall stability of a world (socio-political or drone functionality), ranging from 0 to 100, base 50. Influenced by happiness, housing, amenities, and crime/deviancy.
low_stability_events: Very low stability can cause unrest events.
Below 25 stability for ≥1 year can trigger a Planetary Revolt situation.

enslaved_species_low_stability_modifiers.name: Hunger Strike
enslaved_species_low_stability_modifiers.effects: −50% Slave pop resource output, −50% Slave upkeep

enslaved_species_low_stability_modifiers.name: Slave Riots
enslaved_species_low_stability_modifiers.effects: −1000% Slave pop resource output, −50% Pop growth speed, −50% Army build speed, −50% Planetary infrastructure build speed

stability.sources: Stability can be further modified by many planet/empire effects (not provided in this extract).

crime_and_deviancy.description: Crime (for individualist empires) or Deviancy (for Gestalt Consciousness) measures non‑compliance. Produced by all sapient pops except those undergoing assimilation, scaling from 0 crime at 100% happiness to +2 crime at 0% happiness. Non‑sentient mechanical pops and integrated machine drones do not produce crime/deviancy.
crime_and_deviancy.modifier_mechanics: Every year, if crime/deviancy conditions are met and no modifier was added in the last 10 years, a persistent or temporary modifier may be added (or +15 devastation). All modifiers are removed after 15 years once crime/deviancy drops below 10; this timer is halved for each Enforcer/Hunter‑Seeker Drone job.

persistent_modifiers.name: Drone Deviancy
persistent_modifiers.effects: +15 Deviancy, +100 Deviant Drone jobs (scales +100 per 33 Deviancy)
persistent_modifiers.description: Deviant drones no longer respond to Hive Mind.

persistent_modifiers.name: Drone Corruption
persistent_modifiers.effects: +15 Deviancy, +100 Corrupt Drone jobs (scales +100 per 33 Deviancy)
persistent_modifiers.description: Corrupted drone networks operate outside parameters.

decisions.description: Planet‑wide undertakings that cost resources, unity, and/or influence, and often require construction time. Decisions can add planet modifiers, planetary features, trigger terraforming, remove negative modifiers, or specialize a planet.

terraforming.description: Permanently change a planet's class (e.g., Restore Ecumenopolis, Arcology Project, Repair the Shattered Ring, Dreamscaping, Banish). Some are activated by relics. Most require specific technologies, ascension perks, or DLC. Cannot be used on Habitats, Ring Worlds, Hive Worlds, Nanite Worlds, or Ecumenopolises (except specific cases).
terraforming.note: Terraforming situation decisions (Consume World, Subsume World, Toxify World) start a situation with no cost and cannot be used on empire capital.

modifier_removal.description: Remove negative planet modifiers (Hostile Fauna, Bleak, Hazardous Weather, Wild Storms, Invasive Xenoflora). Require technologies (Terrestrial Sculpting) and cost energy/food.

planetary_feature.description: Add a permanent planetary feature (Nature Preserve blocker, Strip Mine Network, Dayside Solar Farm, Tree of Life Sapling, Progenitor Nest, etc.) with various requirements and DLC.

planet_modifier.description: Apply temporary or permanent modifiers to a planet (Distribute Luxury Goods, Anti‑Crime Campaign, Negotiate with Crime Lords, Expel Excess Population, Mastery of Nature, Cultivated Worldscaping, etc.). Many are unlocked by DLC or special events.

specialized_planets.description: Convert a colony into a specialized designation (Penal Colony, Thrall‑World, Resort World) costing 1000 Unity, replacing districts/buildings and adding unique jobs. Can only have one of each (except Thrall‑Worlds). Cannot be used by Gestalts. Abolishing costs 2500 Unity.

enclave_decisions.description: Purchased from enclaves (Artisan Troupe, Mindwarden, Crystalline Empire). Add temporary or permanent modifiers (Exhibit Art Monument, Thought Police, Crystalline Construction).

minor_artifact_decisions.description: Cost 100 minor artifacts, last 10 years. Provide boosted output for specific job types based on empire type (e.g., Bureaucrats, Executives, Menial Drones, Bio‑Trophies). Not available on Nanite Worlds.

consecrate_world.description: Requires Consecrated Worlds ascension perk. Up to 3 uncolonized planets can be consecrated at 500 Unity each, gaining a quality rating (Holy, Venerated, Respected, Profane) based on planet class, providing monthly unity, amenity bonus, and spiritualist attraction. Bonus cumulative. Colonizing removes the modifier.

planetary_deficits.description: When a world consumes more of a resource than it produces, the logistic toll costs trade value equal to 1/8 of the base market price per missing resource. The trade cost is shown in the trade overview (Consumed) after subtracting the planet's own trade production.

deficit_costs.resource: Exotic Gases, Rare Crystals, Volatile Motes
Dark Matter, Living Metal, Zro, Nanites

hotkeys.description: Keyboard shortcuts for common actions in Stellaris. Most menus and interactions can also be performed via mouse. Hotkeys are not rebindable in vanilla.

navigation_menu.ESC: Close active menu / Open main menu

ship_menu.⇧Shift: Add an action to fleet's action queue
ship_menu.^Ctrl + ⇧Shift: Add an action to the top of a fleet's action queue

starbase_menu.U: Upgrades Starbase (while in starbase menu) / Upgrades Defensive Platforms (while in defense menu)
starbase_menu.B: Build Defensive Platforms (while in defense menu) / Build Ships (while in shipyard menu)

general.RMB: Dismiss notification / Unassign leader / Go To outliner object
Create control group (quick select for worlds/fleets)

camera.RMB + drag: Rotate camera (system/galaxy) / Free rotation around focused object

screenshots.F12: Steam overlay screenshot (JPG lossy). Files via Steam Library > right-click Stellaris > View screenshots

expansion.description: Empires claim unowned systems by building starbase outposts. Outpost cost: 100 Alloys, 75 Influence (increases with distance from owned systems, capped at 1000 Influence), 1 Energy upkeep. Expanding increases Empire Size; each point above 100 adds +0.2% Technology cost and +0.2% Tradition cost. Claim systems sequentially to keep influence costs low, or skip ahead to strategic chokepoints.

outposts_and_starbases.description: Starbases begin as Outposts. Upgrading to Starport → Starhold → Star Fortress → Citadel unlocks buildings and modules (shipyards, gun/missile/hangar batteries, trade hubs, anchorages). Each starbase collects trade value in its system. Starbase capacity is a soft limit; exceeding it sharply increases upkeep of all starbases. FTL Inhibitor technology forces enemy fleets to capture the starbase before they can leave the system via hyperlane. Defense platforms can be added to increase combat power.

colonization.description: Colony ship costs 200 Alloys, 200 Food, 200 Consumer Goods (standard empire) and takes 1 year to build. Landing takes ~3 years (scales with total empire pops). Colony starts with a Reassembled Ship Shelter and 1 pop. Capital building upgrades at 1000, 2500, 5000 pops, unlocking more building slots, specialisation options, and additional leader jobs. Pop growth is slower on new colonies until pop count increases.

empire_size.description: Represents the administrative burden of a large empire. Base size is 100. Additional size comes from colonies, systems, pops, and districts. Each point above 100 increases Technology cost by +0.2% and Tradition cost by +0.2% (additive). Effects can be reduced by certain traditions, technologies, leader traits, and ethics.

habitability.description: Determines how well a species can live on a planet. Base habitability depends on climate match: same class 80%, same climate (frozen/wet/dry) 60%, different climate 20%, homeworld always 100%. For each point below 100%, pop resource output is reduced by −0.5%, pop upkeep (food, consumer goods, amenities) is increased by +0.5%, and pop growth speed is reduced by −0.5%. Modified by species traits, technologies, and traditions.

resource_categories.description: Resources are divided into four tiers: Basic (Energy Credits, Minerals, Food, Trade Value) used for upkeep, construction, and market; Advanced (Consumer Goods, Alloys) used for pop living standards, research, unity jobs, and military construction; Strategic — common (Exotic Gases, Rare Crystals, Volatile Motes) required for upgraded buildings and advanced ship components, rare (Dark Matter, Living Metal, Zro, Nanites) provide specialized bonuses; Abstract (Influence, Unity, Physics/Society/Engineering Research) represent political power, cultural progress, and scientific knowledge.

technology_system.description: Research is split into three branches: Physics, Society, Engineering. Uses a deck‑of‑cards system: each branch draws 3 random technologies the empire meets the prerequisites for; the player picks one. Research speed is modified by scientist expertise (+15% if matching field), ethics, and other bonuses. Unused research points accumulate in a stored pool (no waste). Techs are categorised as starting (always known), acquired (partial progress from debris), rare (purple, low draw weight), dangerous (red, may trigger crises), and repeatable (golden, infinite scaling). Each tech belongs to a theme field (e.g., Computing, Biology, Propulsion).

traditions_system.description: Spend Unity to adopt traditions. There are 14 regular tradition trees and 4 ascension trees (for ascension paths). An empire may select up to 7 trees. Each tree has 5 traditions plus an adoption effect and a finisher. Completing a tree unlocks an Ascension Perk slot. Some trees are locked behind DLC or authority type. Example trees: Expansion, Discovery, Supremacy, Diplomacy.

ascension_perks.description: Powerful bonuses unlocked by completing tradition trees or researching specific technologies. An empire can acquire up to 8 ascension perk slots (with DLC). Perks enable mega‑structures, Colossi, ascension paths, and other transformative abilities, representing the empire's ultimate ambitions.

ascension_paths.description: Four mutually exclusive paths representing the ultimate species evolution: Genetics (advanced traits and modification), Psionics (access the Shroud, covenants), Cybernetics (add cybernetic traits), Synthetics (transform organic pops into robots). Machine empires have three alternative paths: Modularity, Nanotech, Virtuality. Each requires an ascension perk and opens a dedicated ascension tradition tree.

leaders_system.description: Three leader types: Official (diplomats, governors), Scientist (research, science ships), Commander (fleets, armies). Leaders have skill levels 1–10, gained through experience. Recruitment costs Unity. Council positions provide additional empire‑wide bonuses. Governors reduce empire size contribution of governed pops by 2% per skill level. Scientists increase research speed by 2% per level; Commanders increase fleet fire rate by 2% per level.

factions_system.description: Pops with specific ethics join factions. Faction approval (affected by empire policies, ruler ethics, events) produces Unity and modifies the happiness of faction members. Promoting or suppressing a faction costs 1 Influence per month and affects ethics attraction. The political power of a faction depends on the number of supporting pops and their power multipliers; unhappy large factions lower stability.

ethics_system.description: Empires spend 3 ethic points across four axes: Egalitarian–Authoritarian, Xenophobe–Xenophile, Militarist–Pacifist, Materialist–Spiritualist. Moderate ethic = 1 point, fanatic = 2 points (double bonuses & harsher restrictions). Must spend all 3. Ethics dictate available authorities, civics, living standards, faction opinions, and diplomacy. Gestalt Consciousness is a special 3‑point ‘ethic’ for hive minds and machine intelligences; no happiness, no consumer goods, unique civics.

authority_system.description: Government form determining how the ruler is chosen. Democratic: elections every 10 years, large candidate pool, can support a candidate for 100 Unity. Oligarchic: elections every 20 years, restricted pool, emergency election for 250 Unity, choose winner for 2000 Unity. Dictatorial: election on ruler death only, restricted pool, choose winner for 2000 Unity. Imperial: hereditary, no elections. Additional authorities: Hive Mind (immortal ruler, food‑based, no happiness), Machine Intelligence (immortal ruler, energy upkeep for pops), Corporate (like Oligarchic, branch offices on foreign planets, higher empire size penalty).

civics_system.description: Each empire picks 2 civics at the start, and a third can be added after researching Galactic Administration tech. Civics provide unique mechanical bonuses and may be restricted by ethics, authority, or origin. Some civics cannot be added or removed after the game begins. They define the foundational principles of the empire's government.

origins_system.description: An origin determines the starting conditions and backstory of the empire. The default Prosperous Unification gives extra starting pops and a 10‑year bonus to homeworld output. Other origins can drastically alter gameplay (e.g., Void Dwellers start on habitats, Doomsday starts on a doomed world). Most require DLC.

species_traits_system.description: Main species starts with 2 trait points, maximum 5 traits (can be modified later). Positive traits cost points; negative traits grant extra points. Traits affect pop output, leader lifespan, army damage, etc. Opposing traits cannot be picked together. Machine species have separate traits. Lithoids consume minerals, have +50% habitability and +50 years leader lifespan but −25% pop growth speed and leaders start older. Plantoids can have Budding (organic pop assembly).

warfare_system.description: Fleets are built from designed ship classes: Corvette, Frigate, Destroyer, Cruiser, Battleship, Titan, Colossus, Juggernaut. Ships cost Alloys (or Food for organic) and have Energy/Trade upkeep. Naval Capacity is a soft cap (exceeding increases upkeep); Fleet Command Limit is a hard cap per fleet. Ship Designer: sections with weapon and utility slots; power budget. Weapon types: energy (bonus vs armor, weak vs shields), kinetic (bonus vs shields, weak vs armor), explosive (penetrates shields, can be shot down by point‑defense). Defenses: shields (regen out of combat), armor, hull, evasion. Armies (assault/defense) invade or protect planets; defense armies are spawned by soldier/enforcer jobs.

federations_system.description: Multi‑empire alliances created after adopting the Diplomacy tradition ‘The Federation’. Types: Galactic Union (cohesion and crisis damage bonus), Trade League (unique trade policy), Martial Alliance (ship fire rate), Research Cooperative (free research agreement, extra megastructures), Holy Covenant (unity, psionic bonuses), Hegemony (semi‑subject members, president gains extra weight). Federation levels up with cohesion; higher levels unlock laws and a federation fleet.

galactic_community_system.description: Once 3 non‑genocidal empires have established contact with 70% of other empires, they can form the Galactic Community. Members vote on resolutions using Diplomatic Weight (derived from fleet power, economy, tech, population, envoys). After 20 years, a Galactic Council of the top 3 members can be created. A council member can become Galactic Custodian (temp powers), and later propose to form the Galactic Imperium, making the custodian the permanent Emperor.

crisis_system.description: Endgame crises are galaxy‑wide threats that appear after the End‑Game Start Year. Possible crises: Contingency (ancient AI), Extradimensional Invaders (Unbidden), Prethoryn Scourge (biological invaders), and the Synthetic Queen (from The Machine Age DLC). Strength is configurable. Awakened Fallen Empires and the War in Heaven (two awakened empires fighting) are additional late‑game threats.

celestial_objects.description: Large bodies within systems: stars, planets, moons, asteroids. They may have resources harvestable by orbital stations. Systems have 2–15 celestial objects.

survey.entry_reveal: When any owned ship enters a system or passes within sensor range, habitable planets and their world type are revealed.
survey.science_ship_survey: Surveying reveals orbital resources, planet size, available districts, planetary features, blockers, and habitability for each species in the empire. May also reveal anomalies.

planet_size.description: Determines the visual size of a planet and the maximum number of districts a habitable planet can support.

habitable_planets_regular.description: Nine regular habitable planet types, divided into three climate categories: Dry, Wet, Frozen. Volcanic Worlds (Infernals DLC) form a fourth category. Each climate has a slight bias towards different planetary features.
habitable_planets_regular.notes: A planet's type affects which planetary features and blockers can appear. Habitability can be modified by species traits, planetary features, and planetary modifiers.

habitable_planets_special.description: Special habitable planets spawn in specific systems or can be created through terraforming, ascension perks, decisions, or origins. Several types have 'Ideal' (always 100%) habitability.

types.name: Gaia
preexisting: Holy Guardians' holy worlds (special Holy World modifier; colonizing angers them, destroying/terraforming awakens them)
types.notes: Gaia Worlds tend to have positive modifiers and no negative ones.

types.name: Tomb
types.notes: Colonizing displeases Traditionalist faction unless Post-Apocalyptic origin or Memorialists civic. Each Tomb World has a high chance of triggering one of 8 unique events 2–3 years after colonization.

types.name: Relic
types.notes: Relic Worlds contain unique planetary features that grant bonuses to research output. Surface covered by expensive-to-remove blockers.

types.name: Ecumenopolis
types.notes: All normal planetary features removed. Cannot be terraformed into other types. Only Arcology districts available. City lights determined by terraforming empire's ship appearance.

types.name: Hive
types.notes: Only habitable by Hive-Minded species or Necrophage Hive Mind. Only Urban districts. All normal planetary features and several modifiers (Atmospheric Aphrodisiac, Atmospheric Hallucinogen, Bleak, Hazardous Weather, Hostile Fauna, Irradiated, Lush, Natural Beauty, Wild Storms) are removed.

types.name: Machine
types.notes: Only habitable by Machine, Mechanical, Cybernetic, or Cyber Commandos pops. Only Urban districts. All normal planetary features and the same modifiers as Hive Worlds are removed. If biological pops without Cybernetic trait are present when terraformed, they are killed and an Organic Slurry planetary feature is added.

types.name: Nanite
types.notes: Only Urban districts. Uncolonizable variants exist in the L-Cluster (with Terraforming Candidate modifier after factory destruction) and in Synthetic Queen's home system (also with Terraforming Candidate modifier). Both require DLCs.

habitable_megastructures.description: Artificial celestial objects constructable after researching required technology. They have the same habitability for every species, different districts and designations, no natural planetary features but may have blockers. Appearance depends on constructor's ship style.

types.name: Ring World
construction: Mega-Engineering technology to repair broken fragments or restore Shattered Ring Worlds
Ring World technology to build from scratch

types.name: Shattered Ring World
effects: Enables the Repair the Shattered Ring decision

types.name: Orbital Habitat
preexisting: Knights of the Toxic God origin home system

types.name: Synaptic Lathe
types.notes: Uses a separate set of buildings. All pops have Neural Chips jobs. Pops are purged regardless of species rights. Cannot grow pops. Cannot have branch offices.

uninhabitable_planets.description: Non-star celestial objects that cannot harbor life. Cannot be colonized or terraformed unless they have the Terraforming Candidate modifier, but may have resource deposits.

special_types.name: AI
special_types.notes: Created by Contingency. Successful bombardment turns into broken world.

special_types.name: Infested
special_types.notes: Created when habitable worlds are infested by Prethoryn Swarm. Successful bombardment turns into barren (terraformable) world.

special_types.name: Shattered
special_types.notes: Created by Terravore consumption or World Cracker colossus. Deposit of 4–16 Minerals.

special_types.name: Shielded
special_types.notes: Created by Global Pacifier colossus or found through exploration. Shield can be removed via special project with various outcomes.

special_types.name: Shrouded
special_types.notes: Created by Teachers of the Shroud origin, Eater of Worlds, End of the Cycle, The Reckoning, or found in special systems.

special_types.name: Cracked
special_types.notes: Created by the hatching of the Voidspawn. Deposit of 20 Society research.

special_types.name: Junk Moon
special_types.notes: Created by an event. Deposit of 10 Alloys and 3 Minerals.

asteroid_belts.description: Appear only in single-star systems or special systems. Each single-star system has a 30% chance for 1 belt, 10% for 2 belts, 2% for 4 belts. Each belt has 1–6 asteroids. Come in 3 types.

asteroid_types.notes: Only in special systems. Home system of Cosmic Dawn origin may have them with Minerals deposits only.

asteroid_belts.survey_speed: Asteroids survey in 15 days (half of normal 30 days).

stars.description: Celestial objects at the center of star systems, classified by spectral characteristics. Influence system generation, habitable planet chance, ship modifiers in system, and available megastructures. Effects stack in binary/trinary systems. All binary/trinary systems except F/M, B/M, B/neutron, A/neutron have 100% habitable planet chance. Natural systems have 4–10 orbiting objects, except neutron stars, black holes, pulsars which have 1–4.

star_types.notes: Can only appear in binary/trinary systems.

other: Unlocks Black Hole Observatory starbase building
Cannot have gas giants in orbit

star_types.notes: Unique to the system created at Quest for the Toxic God completion.
Unique to the azilash system. Celestial objects orbit an ancient Astral Rift instead of a physical star.

astral_scars.description: Celestial objects at the edge of systems, added by the Astral Planes DLC. Initially 10% of systems contain one with a deposit of 2 Astral Threads. After the mid-game year, each year there is a small chance for new scars to appear and for existing ones to become an astral rift. New scars have 1 Astral Threads deposit.

spawn_rules.post_midgame: Chance each year; twice as likely in multi-star systems, thrice as likely in nebulae
spawn_rules.proximity_rule: Cannot appear if an adjacent system already has an astral scar or rift
spawn_rules.rift_conversion: Existing scars may become astral rifts after mid-game year

megastructures.description: Colossal end-game constructions that provide large bonuses and demonstrate technological primacy. They cannot be built over celestial bodies with uninvestigated anomalies. All megastructures except Gateways are destroyed if a Star-Eater destroys the system.

general_rules.initial_stage: Requires a construction ship; later stages do not.
general_rules.build_speed: Base progress 1/day, modified by various sources (Architectural Renaissance edict, Living Metal edict, Megastructure Construction speed repeatable tech, etc.). A stage requiring 3600 days can finish faster.
general_rules.parallel_construction: An empire can only build, upgrade, or restore one multi-stage megastructure at a time. This limit is increased by perks/techs (Master Builders AP, Architectural Renaissance edict, Mega-Engineering tech).
general_rules.build_limit: Most multi-stage megastructures have a galaxy-wide or empire-wide limit (usually 1 per type). Conquered or restored ruined ones don't count against the limit. The Isolated Contingency Core relic allows +1 of each type.
general_rules.system_restrictions: Only one multi-stage megastructure per system. Must be built over uninhabitable planets or stars without anomalies. Specific megastructures have further requirements (e.g., Dyson Sphere not in binary systems, Matter Decompressor only around black holes).
general_rules.ruined_megastructures: Can be repaired mid-game with Mega-Engineering technology, often at reduced cost. Repairing them does not count towards build limits, but also does not grant achievements. Owning a system with a ruined megastructure gives ×20 weight for Mega-Engineering tech to appear.
general_rules.visual: Many megastructures change appearance based on the shipset of the constructing empire.

ftl_megastructures.description: Built at the edge of a system, allow fleets to traverse to an identical megastructure. Work across empires unless borders are closed or at war. Can be built in subjects' systems. Can be dismantled if at peace and have the tech.

gateways.description: Abandoned gateways spawn per galaxy settings; Galactic Doorstep/Imperial Fiefdom origins guarantee at least one. Repair requires Gateway Activation tech, 6000 energy, 2500 alloys, 2 years. Activating the first gateway also activates another random one. Gateways allow instant travel to any other gateway. They act like hyperlane connections for claims and trade. New gateways can be built with Gateway Construction tech in two stages: Site (2500 alloys, 75 influence, 3 years) then Gateway (2500 alloys, 6000 energy, 5 years).
