Biological shipset alternatives: food replaces half the alloy cost.
gateways.special: L-Gates (Distant Stars) are a separate network leading to the L-Cluster Terminal Egress system. Opened via special project, cannot be constructed.
gateways.note: Repairing a gateway has a 20% chance to discover the legendary paragon Skrand Sharpbeak (50% on subsequent repairs or if Skravird discovered; Galactic Doorstep origin has 33%/67%).

hyper_relays.description: Requires Overlord DLC and Hyper Relays technology. Allows ships to jump between adjacent Hyper Relays, bypassing sublight travel across systems. Built in one stage: 25 influence, 100 rare crystals, 500 alloys (mechanical) or 250 alloys + 875 food (biological). Build time 1 year. Dismantling costs 500 energy (Scavengers civic gains 50 alloys, 10 rare crystals).
hyper_relays.effects: Hyper Relay edicts require the empire capital to be part of the network. Subject specialization benefits require a connection between overlord and subject capitals. Hyper Relays do not connect through wormholes or gateways; manual placement near them is recommended.
hyper_relays.network_effects: Various edicts and subject specializations grant bonuses (e.g., +stability, -crime, +trade protection) when connected.

habitats.description: Artificial colonizable worlds built above stars or planets. Requires Orbital Habitats tech; takes 5 years. Cost: 200 influence plus 1500 alloys (mechanical) or 750 alloys + 2625 food (biological). One habitat per system; cannot be built in ringworld systems, above moons, or on objects with existing megastructures. Colonization develops twice as fast.
habitats.districts: Max districts = 0.25 per planet/star in system per habitat tier. Resource district slots depend on deposits: each deposit gives (1 + half its value) districts, rounded down. Building a resource district creates an orbital on a matching celestial body, replacing existing mining/research stations. Housing buildings only available to Gestalt Consciousness, Void Dwellers, or empires with Voidborne AP.
sources: Utopia DLC: any empire can research habitat techs.
Void Dwellers origin: starts with a habitat and tech; can upgrade without Utopia.
Knights of the Toxic God (Toxoids): starts with a habitat; can build more but cannot upgrade without Utopia.
Payback origin (First Contact): can gain a size 10 'Recovered Asset' habitat; unlocks build tech but not upgrade without Utopia.
Apocalypse DLC: empires formed from the Horde breaking up start with habitat tech.
Federation's End unique system: can spawn with two habitats (no celestial bodies for districts).
Ithome cluster (First Contact): 25% chance to spawn with 3 habitats, each with unique modifiers giving max habitability and extra districts.
Cosmic Storms DLC: habitat obtainable from Hold the Line archaeological site.

deep_space_citadels.description: Large defensive stations that can mount starbase buildings and defense platforms. Normally exclusive to Fallen Empires; with BioGenesis DLC all empires can research the tech to build them. Designed in ship designer. Built at tier I and upgraded. System can have one deep space citadel; increased by +1 per: Mega-Engineering tech, Eternal Vigilance AP, Starlit Citadel origin.

orbital_rings.description: Requires Overlord DLC. Built around colonized planets; acts like an additional starbase. Constructed in one stage; upgraded to tier II/III like starbases. Have +100% fire rate. Modules and buildings provide bonuses to the planet.

tiers.requirements: Star Fortress tech, Tier II+ capital or Ascension 3
Citadel tech, Tier III+ capital or Ascension 5

orbital_rings.modules: Use own set. Orbital Assembly Complex holding on planet provides bonuses. Key modules: Anchorage (+5 naval cap), Vivarium Tank (+50 vivarium capacity), Habitation Module (+1 max district, requires Anti-Gravity Engineering). Fleet modules (Shipyard, Hatchery, Beastport) cost 50 alloys, 180 days, 1 energy upkeep; produce 2 ships at once with Orbital Assembly Complex. Defense modules (Guns, Batteries, Hangars) cost 120 alloys, 180 days, 1 energy upkeep; add weapon slots and platform cap.
orbital_rings.buildings: Special buildings that boost planet output. All cost alloys + strategic resource, take 360 days. Examples: Climate Optimization Stations (+0.5 food per 100 farmers), Low Gravity Mega-Refiners (+0.5 minerals per 100 miners), Stratospheric Ionization Elements (+0.5 energy per 100 technicians), Alloy Processing Facilities (+0.5 alloys per 100 metallurgists, -2 minerals), Orbital Logistics Systems (+0.5 consumer goods per 100 artisans, -1 minerals), The Giga-Mall (+1 amenities per trader, +5% trade), etc.
orbital_rings.planetary_replacements: Some orbital ring buildings replace planet buildings: Orbital Embassy Complex (+100 politicians, +2 envoys, capital), Orbital Stock Exchange (+400 traders, +10% trade), Orbital Psi Corps (+200 telepaths, +2 stability), Noble Estates (+200 nobles, +200 housing), Orbital Slave Processing Hub (+5% slave output, -25% slave political power).
orbital_rings.ruined: Ruined by crisis; can be rebuilt if planet is reclaimed.

multi_stage_megastructures.description: Constructed in multiple stages, each requiring the matching technology. Start with a site providing no benefit; each stage increases bonuses. Only one per system; must be over uninhabitable planet or star. Simultaneous builds limited as per general rules.

stages.name: Stage IV (Groik Nexus)
stages.note: Fountain of Youth event chain possible upgrade.

stages.name: I
stages.production: 20 sensor range, +10 base intel, -5% hyperdrive charge

stages.name: II
stages.production: 40 sensor range, +20 base intel, -10% hyperdrive charge, +1 codebreaking

stages.name: III
stages.production: 60 sensor range, +30 base intel, -15% hyperdrive charge, +1 codebreaking

stages.name: IV
stages.production: Entire Galaxy sensor range, +40 base intel, -20% hyperdrive charge, +2 codebreaking

sentry_array.note: Completed array prevents space creature respawning. Can be obtained from Ultima Vigilis archaeological site.

stages.name: Segments 1-4
stages.note: Each segment adds 1 habitable segment with 100% habitability, up to 10 districts. Segments can be ruined by Colossus/crisis.

ring_world.requirements: Cannot build around black holes, in systems with multiple stars, or with habitable planets/megastructures. Upon frame completion, all planets are removed. Habitable segments require colonization.
ring_world.natural_spawns: Up to 8 ringworlds can spawn: Sanctuary (4 intact, pre-FTLs), Cybrex Alpha (ruined, requires precursor chain), Cybrex Beta (revealed after Contingency defeat), random ruined, Shattered Ring origin systems, Ancient Caretaker systems.

stages.name: I (Hull)
stages.production: +100 naval cap, +2 starbase cap, +4 defense platforms, +5% sublight speed

stages.name: II (Comms)
stages.production: +200 naval cap, +4 starbase cap, +8 platforms, +10% speed

stages.name: III (Completed)
stages.production: +300 naval cap, +6 starbase cap, +12 platforms, +15% speed

stages.name: II
stages.production: +20% diplo weight, +30 opinion, +1 envoy, -5% empire size

stages.name: III
stages.production: +30% diplo weight, +75 opinion, +1 envoy, -5% empire size

stages.name: IV
stages.production: +40% diplo weight, +150 opinion, +2 envoys, -10% empire size

interstellar_assembly.note: All values stack if multiple owned.

matter_decompressor.requirements: Only around black holes; no habitable planets/anomalies. Removes existing research station on black hole.

stages.name: III (Complete)
stages.production: +20 shipyard cap, +100% build speed, Titan/Colossal construction

mega_shipyard.requirements: Only around stars in unary systems; no pulsars/neutron stars/black holes. All ships start with 100 experience.

quantum_catapult.requirements: Only around pulsars/neutron stars. Catapulted fleets gain +33% fire rate for 120 days (+50% with Slingshot to the Stars origin). Quantum Catapult tech required to use. Hyper Relays tech required for MIA reduction. Accuracy decreases with distance.
quantum_catapult.slingshot_origin: Slingshot to the Stars empires start with ruined catapult that can be restored without Mega-Engineering (but need it to upgrade). Restoration costs 2500 alloys at stage I, 10000 alloys at stage II. Catapulted fleets get +50% fire rate.

stages.name: I (Equatorial Band)
stages.production: +25% mining station output, +1 minerals on eligible bodies

stages.name: II (Borehole Pumps)
stages.production: +50% mining output, +2 minerals on eligible bodies

stages.name: III (Mohole Extractors)
stages.production: +75% mining output, +2 minerals, +1 alloys on eligible bodies

stages.name: IV (Completed)
stages.production: +100% mining output, +2 minerals, +2 alloys on eligible bodies

arc_furnace.requirements: Only around molten worlds. Cannot build in systems with Galactic Crucible. Does not add deposits to stars/astral scars/colonizable planets/research deposit bodies. Trade deposits are removed. Mega-Engineering increases build limit by 2. Arc Welders origin starts with stage I and can build +1 above limit. Can be dismantled for 500 energy per stage if at peace. Ancient Mining Drones home system may spawn ruined Arc Furnace.

dyson_swarms_and_sphere.description: Dyson Swarms (The Machine Age DLC) and Dyson Sphere (Utopia DLC) are linked if both DLCs are enabled. Dyson Sphere cannot be built in binary/trinary or around pulsars/neutron stars/black holes.

dyson_swarm.dismantle: 500 energy per stage if at peace and have tech. Upgrading to Dyson Sphere removes it from Swarm limit.

dyson_sphere.requirements: Requires Galactic Wonders AP and Dyson Sphere tech (Utopia). If The Machine Age is active, must be built by upgrading a completed Dyson Swarm (starts at Stage I Initial and inherits swarm effects). If Machine Age is not active, built from scratch starting at Site. Ruined Dyson Sphere is always restored to stage V (Completed) and only requires Mega-Engineering.
dyson_sphere.effects: Cannot build in systems with pre-FTL unless Aggressive Interference. Prevents colonization. Upon completion, rocky planets become frozen/cold barren worlds. Mining station removed. Terraforming Candidate modifier removed.

aetherophasic_engine.description: Nemesis DLC. Appears in capital system upon reaching final Galactic Nemesis crisis level. Each stage requires dark matter and 10 years. If conquered, becomes ruined and can be repaired by original owner. Other crisis empires can take control.

stages.reward_if_conquered: 200 influence, 5000 alloys, 10000 dark matter (lvl5 nemesis only)

stages.effect: Within 1-2 years, 5% owned systems invaded by shroud avatars.
Up to 600 psionic pops and 2 psychic leaders die. 10% systems invaded.

galactic_crucible.description: Infernals DLC. Requires Galactic Hyperthermia AP and special project completion (or Red Giant origin). Built in single star system without colonies/Arc Furnace, not around pulsar/neutron star/black hole. One per empire.

stages.name: Hub
stages.note: Requires Designated Firestorm project; mutually exclusive with Institute.

stages.name: Galactic Crucible
stages.note: Requires Designated Firestorm; mutually exclusive with Institute.

institute_path.name: Galactic Crucible Institute
institute_path.note: Requires Unforeseen Correlation project; mutually exclusive with Hub/Crucible.

galactic_crucible.overclocking: Available at crisis level 2. Modes: None (+5 crystallized entropy, -10 alloys), Overcharged (-20 days conduit construction), Production (+50 crystallized entropy, -45 alloys), Coordination Center (+20% home territory fire rate).
galactic_crucible.conquest: If new owner has Thermophile founder species, gains 150-100000 unity. Otherwise, can destroy (gains alloys/unity/dark matter) or convert to Institute.

synaptic_lathe.description: The Machine Age DLC. Requires Scalable Reservoir Computing tech (from Cosmogenesis AP). Costs 15000 alloys (mech) / 7500 alloys + 26250 food (bio), 300 influence, 2400 days. Only one. Functions like a planet with pops, jobs (Neural Chip), stability, housing, amenities, designation, ascension. Stability below threshold still affects job output but no revolts.
synaptic_lathe.mechanics: Pops are purged over time regardless of species rights; rate scales with population. If population reaches zero, owner can refill with 100 pops or let it break. Virtual pops produce no resources. Pops can be resettled in (but not out). Last pop cannot leave. Synaptic Lathe has no defenses; surrenders if enemy fleet enters orbit. If conqueror doesn't have Cosmogenesis AP, megastructure disabled. On occupation, options: resettle half pops to capital, gain research (500-1000000) with -75 decaying opinion, or do nothing. Cosmogenesis empires can keep it. Renouncing crisis path destroys it permanently.
synaptic_lathe.note: Low stability does not create revolts but still reduces output.

grand_archive.description: Grand Archive DLC. Requires Galactic Archivism tech. Can only be built over worlds with ≥2500 pops (unless Wilderness origin). Built in one stage; base upkeep 10 energy. Cost: 1000 alloys (mech) / 500 alloys + 500 food (bio); -25% with Galactic Curators civic. 1800 days. Enables exhibit specimens, +50 Vivarium Capacity. Completing it grants +10 opinion with Curator Order if known, and scaled unity for Treasure Hunters origin or Galactic Curators/Beastmasters civics.
grand_archive.limit: One per empire. Integrating a subject with one removes it. On conquest, megastructure destroyed; space fauna released; conqueror steals 1 specimen per category (2 for Barbaric Despoilers) and gains 100 minor artifacts (Ancient Relics). Dismantling starbase removes Archive; refunds 500 alloys, excess fauna culled.

behemoth_megastructures.description: BioGenesis DLC. Requires Behemoth Fury AP; available at crisis level 2. Used to hatch behemoths from eggs. Egg types: Standard (50000 food, 300 influence, 10 years; hatch with 100000 food, 150 influence, 10 years -> Class I Behemoth; Chrysalis upgrades to II/III with feeding), Strengthened (10000 food, 2 months; hatch 90000 food, 2 years -> I), Rapid (10000 food, 2 months; hatch 10000 food, 6 months -> size 32 fleet), Large (10000 food, 2 months; hatch 40000 food, 1 year -> size 96 fleet). Eggs destroyed if enraged behemoth reaches them.

shroud_seal.description: Shadows of the Shroud DLC. Requires Psionic Suppression tech. Cost: 500 alloys, 100 Zro; 3 years. Reduces Psionic Aura intensity by 10/day in system, 1/day in adjacent systems; halves fire rate of psionic weapons. Upkeep 5 energy. The Dreamer relic adds +25 unity, +10 naval cap per seal and extends range to 2 hyperlanes. AI psionic empires dismantle them. Completing first seal after meeting Shroudwalkers loses -5 opinion. Mindwardens enclave system always has one; their raids place one. Cosmic Storms DLC: Shroud Seal makes Shroud Storms 75% less likely in borders.

buildings.description: Buildings are the third level of world development, requiring both districts and specializations to be constructed. Buildings may be ruined by orbital bombardment or collateral damage. Ruined buildings can be repaired for half of their cost and construction time. A total of 21 buildings may be built on a planet.

building_sets.description: Each building belongs to one or more building sets. A building can only be built in a district that permits one of its building sets and does not exclude any of them. Changing specialization may remove incompatible buildings.
building_sets.general_slots: Five general building slots available on all planets, plus Urban Expansion specialization. Most specializations can accept automation buildings (planet limit 1).
building_sets.extraction_rule: Extraction buildings can only be built in relevant extraction districts.
building_sets.civilian_industry: Accepts consumer goods buildings and rare resource refineries.
building_sets.mixed_industry: Accepts both civilian and heavy industry buildings.
building_sets.research_specializations: Accept research buildings; Archives accept research and unity; Administrative Hubs accept unity only.
building_sets.agrarian_idyll: Agrarian Villages specializations accept 'urban' buildings including energy and mineral extraction.

capital.description: Capital always occupies the first building slot. Provides housing, amenities, defense armies, crime reduction jobs, and empire-specific jobs. Cannot be demolished, downgraded, disabled, or ruined. Automatically converts if planet traded/conquered by different authority or designation changes.

housing.description: Two tiers, increase housing and amenities. Cannot be built on Penal Colony or Thrall-World. Converted if conquered by empire with different building line.

types.name: Luxury Residences
requirements: Not Habitat (unless Void Dwellers or Voidborne)

types.name: Paradise Domes
requirements: Not Habitat (unless Void Dwellers or Voidborne)

types.name: Communal Housing
requirements: Not Habitat (unless Void Dwellers or Voidborne)

types.name: Utopian Communal Housing
requirements: Not Habitat (unless Void Dwellers or Voidborne)

law_enforcement.description: Two tiers, provide Enforcer or equivalent jobs generating defense armies and unity while reducing crime/deviancy. Converted if conquered by empire with different building line.

unity.description: Buildings that produce Unity. Converted if conquered by empire with different building line. Rogue Servitor buildings convert to Housing buildings instead.

types.name: Administrative Offices
requirements: Not A Union of Faith federation perk

types.name: Temple
requirements: Spiritualist OR Fanatic Spiritualist OR A Union of Faith federation perk

types.name: Sacrificial Temple
requirements: Death Cult OR Corporate Death Cult civic

research.description: Three tiers, provide research jobs for all fields. Can only be built in specializations that allow research buildings.

types.name: Advanced Research Complexes
effects: +1 Dimensional Portal Researcher if Extradimensional Experimentation modifier

foundry.description: Three tiers, provide Metallurgist jobs producing alloys.

Wilderness origin pays +400 Biomass.

weapon_components.description: Weapons attach to military ship slots (Small, Medium, Large, Extra-Large, Guided/Torpedo, Hangar, Point-Defense, Titan). Larger sizes have more damage and range but less tracking. Weapons have damage multipliers against shield/armor/hull and may have penetration values.

stats.cost: Alloys (or Food for biological, Living Metal for special), strategic resources
stats.accuracy: Base hit chance vs 0% evasion target

strategic_resources.Minor Artifacts: Archaeotech weapons (+33% damage with Archaeo-Engineers, +15% with Rubricator)

biological_vs_mechanical.small_weapons: Biological: higher damage, shorter range than mechanical
biological_vs_mechanical.large_xl_weapons: Biological: longer cooldown, reduced damage/range than mechanical
biological_vs_mechanical.strike_craft: Biological: 2x units per hangar, 0.5x damage each, armor instead of shields, lower hull

energy_weapons.category: Anti-armor and anti-hull focused, weak vs shields

ancient_cavitation_collapser.description: Archaeotech laser with armor penetration, +48% damage with Archaeo-Engineers+Rubricator

plasma_launchers.description: High anti-armor/anti-hull, extremely weak vs shields, L has 45-80 range

lances.biological_variant: Beams: 14.9 day cooldown, 0-120 range, reduced damage

cutting_laser.description: Salvaged from mining drones, T2-equivalent anti-hull weapon

matter_disintegrator.description: Salvaged from Extradimensional Invaders, short range, anti-hull/armor

disruptors.on_hit: Reduces target shield/armor/hull regen efficiency for 10 days
disruptors.description: Penetrating weapons, damage directly to hull, reduces regen

psionic_disruptor.on_hit: -20% ship fire rate for 10 days, -35% shield regen

cloud_lightning.description: Salvaged from Void Clouds, pure penetration but low damage

psionic_lightning.on_hit: 25-10% chance to terrify target for 5-10 days (based on ship size)

arc_emitter.description: XL penetrating weapon, very high damage, long range
arc_emitter.biological_variant: Lightning Emitter: 15.1 day cooldown, 10-120 range, reduced damage

energy_siphon.description: Society tech, salvaged from Tiyanki, anti-shield specialist

null_void_beam.description: From null void event, extreme anti-shield, very long range

mass_drivers.biological_variant: Shard Accelerators: S has shorter range (0-25) with more damage, L has longer cooldown

ancient_macro_batteries.description: Archaeotech, 50% longer range, L matches X-slot range (0-150)

autocannons.description: High DPS, very short range (0-30/35/40), excellent for ambush

nanite_variant.name: Nanite Autocannon
nanite_variant.dlc: Cosmogenesis Crisis Level V or Scavenger Bot salvage

kinetic_launchers.biological_variant: Chitin Battery: longer cooldown (10.65), shorter range (45-96), reduced damage

reshaping_projectile_accelerator.description: Anti-armor kinetic, costs Living Metal instead of Alloys

mega_cannons.description: XL spinal mount, extreme range anti-shield

ancient_saturator_artillery.description: Archaeotech X-slot, unlocks before Battleships, anti-shield/hull focus
ancient_saturator_artillery.biological_variant: Ancient Fragmentor Artillery: 17 day cooldown, reduced stats

mandibles.description: Replaces torpedoes for bioships, very short range, fast cooldown

explosive_weapons.category: Guided projectiles that travel to target, can be shot down by point-defense

missiles.description: Long range (0-100), shield bypass, vulnerable to PD

ancient_nano_missile_cloud.description: Archaeotech, extremely fast fire rate, hull damage, PD swarm

swarmer_missiles.description: Very PD-resistant (20 hp, 10 armor per missile), long range (0-120)
swarmer_missiles.biological_variant: Can use G-slot on Harbingers, reduced shield pen (50%), +50% armor damage

torpedoes.description: Short range (0-30), massive anti-armor damage vs capital ships, very slow

strike_craft.mechanics: Launch from carriers, regenerate at rate shown, can exceed capacity in combat, can be shot down
strike_craft.biological_variant: 16 units per hangar (vs 8), 1 regen/Time (vs 0.5), armor instead of shields

amoeba_flagella.description: Anti-armor strike craft, low evasion, no shields

ancient_driller_drones.description: Armor-penetrating, weak vs shields, effective vs Prethoryn/cloaked fleets

psionic_bombers.description: Torpedo-like strike craft, weak tracking vs small ships

weaver_weapons.description: Biological-only support/suppressor weapons using PD slots

confusion_spores.on_hit: Chance to cause friendly fire for 5-10 days

point_defense.description: Destroy incoming missiles and strike craft, can attack ships at short range

ancient_defensive_web_slinger.description: Slower fire rate than Guardian PD, +25% damage per hit

titanic_weapons.description: Single weapon per Titan/Ion Cannon, splash damage to nearby ships

perdition_beam.on_hit: 500 damage to ships within 40 range (Titan), 20 range (Ion Cannon)

ancient_ruination_glare.on_hit: 500 damage to ships within 40 range (Titan), 20 range (Ion Cannon)
ancient_ruination_glare.description: Faster fire rate than Perdition Beam, anti-armor focus

unobtainable_weapons.description: Used by space fauna and crises, cannot be researched by players

voidworm_fangs.description: Short-range bite, extreme damage to shields and armor

ship_components.description: Ship components are selected in the ship designer. Most are required; some slots are optional. Excess power increases evasion, speed, and damage slightly.

dlc_notes.biogenesis: Biological ships use metabolisms/organs instead of reactors/thrusters, cost Food (3.5× alloy cost), grow through stages (Juvenile→Mature→Elder, +25%/+50% output).
dlc_notes.grand_archive: Space fauna use bio-components, cost Food/Minerals depending on clone type (Tiyanki/Amoeba: Food; Crystalline/Cutholoid: Minerals; Voidworm: half each).
dlc_notes.apocalypse: Titan field emitters require Apocalypse, The Machine Age, or BioGenesis DLC.
dlc_notes.federations: Juggernaut field emitters require Federations or BioGenesis DLC.

power_generation.description: Provides power for other components. Designs cannot be saved with negative power.

ftl.description: Optional component. Without it, ships cannot move between systems. Cannot be reverse-engineered from debris. Jump drives allow tactical jumps within range (yellow dotted circle), followed by 200-day recharge with −50% sublight speed and weapons damage.

space_fauna.note: Food for Tiyanki/Amoeba; Minerals for Crystalline/Cutholoid; half each for Voidworm

computer_system.description: Improves fire-rate and combat stats based on behavior type. Ships advance to engagement range based on role and weapon range.

behaviors.Swarm: Charge at enemies, fight from shortest weapon range.
behaviors.Picket: Advance to medium range, intercept enemies.
behaviors.Line: Advance to medium range, hold formation.
behaviors.Artillery: Stay at maximum range, fire longest-range weapons.
behaviors.Carrier: Stay at maximum hangar engagement range.
behaviors.Platform: Stationary, fire at enemies in range.

weaver_neural_implants.description: Weavers use special neural implants with Suppressor or Support roles.

effects.suppressor: +5% Weapon Fire Rate, +5% Chance to hit
+10% Weapon Fire Rate, +10% Chance to hit
+20% Weapon Fire Rate, +20% Chance to hit

levels.name: Precognitive

effects.suppressor: +15% Weapon Fire Rate, +15% Chance to hit, +10 Tracking

levels.support: +15% Weapon Fire Rate, +15% Evasion, +20% Sublight speed

propulsion.description: Determines sublight speed and evasion. Biological ships use stellar motor organs.

sensors.description: Determines detection range on galaxy map and increases weapon tracking.

field_emitters.description: Apply buffs to allied ships or debuffs to enemies. Multiple instances do not stack.

standard.name: FTL Inhibitor
effects: Enemy ships can only exit via entry hyperlane

standard.name: Electromagnetic Disruptor
standard.target: hostiles (Sapient/Autonomous computers or Machine founder species)

titan.requires_dlc: Apocalypse, The Machine Age, or BioGenesis

supplementary_organs.description: Biological ship components unlocked by development technology. Cost Food; tier 1 requires 5 power, tier 2 requires 10 power.

components.name: Developmental Pheromones
effects: +1 Growth Progress Rate for Biological Ships in fleet
+50% Upkeep for Biological Ships in fleet

components.name: Exotic Developmental Pheromones
effects: +2 Growth Progress Rate for Biological Ships in fleet
+100% Upkeep for Biological Ships in fleet

ship_utility_components.description: Utility components enhance ship survivability. Unlocked by researching or obtaining matching technology. Same build cost for mechanical and biological ships; upkeep varies by shipset. Components come in Small, Medium, and Large sizes with scaling stats.

shields.description: First layer of hit points. Effective vs energy weapons, vulnerable to kinetic weapons, penetrated by guided weapons and strike craft. Regenerate but consume power. Hardening reduces damage from bypass weapons.

components.name: Dark Matter Shield
components.source: Reverse-engineer Fallen/Awakened Empire ships, Cosmogenesis progression, or Dark Matter Breakthrough agenda

components.name: Psionic Barrier
components.notes: Not nullified by Psi-Phase Field Generator cloaking. Cannot reverse-engineer.

components.name: Psionic Shield
components.notes: Not nullified by Psi-Phase Field Generator cloaking. Cannot reverse-engineer.

components.name: Suspension Field
components.requires: Archaeo-Engineers ascension perk for +60% shield hit points

armor.description: Second layer of hit points. Effective vs kinetic weapons, vulnerable to energy weapons. ~33% more HP than equivalent shield tier. Does not regenerate (except Galvanic variants or with repair components).

galvanic_variants.cost: Living Metal equal to 2× alloy cost (upkeep still alloys)
effects: Biological: Armor regen doubled, regenerates to 100% instead of 50%

components.name: Dragonscale
components.source: Defeat space dragon, gift from Cetana, or Cosmogenesis progression

components.name: Pulse Armor
components.requires: Archaeo-Engineers ascension perk doubles shield hit points

plating.description: Increases hull points. Acquired from Crystalline Entities (first contact or reverse-engineering) or Caravaneers abandoned ship.

auxiliary.description: Go in Auxiliary slots. Varied effects. Do not increase ship upkeep except cloaking fields.

regeneration.name: Regenerative Hull Tissue
regeneration.note: Effect ×5 out of combat. In-game display bug: actual rate is 1/100 shown value.

regeneration.name: Nanite Repair System
regeneration.requires_dlc: Distant Stars or The Machine Age

marks.requires_dlc: Utopia or Shadows of the Shroud
marks.description: Unlocked by forming a Covenant with a Shroud major patron. With only Utopia, also needs maximum Attunement.

cloaking_devices.description: Allow ships to activate cloaking. One per ship. Nullifies shields (except Psionic variants with Psi-Phase Field Generator). Cost does not apply to nanite or menacing ships.
cloaking_devices.mechanics: Cloaking Strength determines detection threshold and penalties. Higher strength = fewer penalties to sublight speed and hyperdrive charge time.

components.name: Psi-Phase Field Generator
components.shield_nullification: Psionic Shields/Barrier keep shields, all others 100%

boarding_cables.description: Unlocked after defeating second Black Needle fleet. 5% chance to capture enemy ship, 0.5% chance to capture Commander. Titans and colossal ships cannot be captured. Nanite/offspring ships only capturable if empire can build them.

commander_capture_options.all_empires: Imprison for +100 Intel on owner's empire

boarding_cables.ransom: Owner can pay Energy scaled to total Trade Value to recover Commander.
boarding_cables.captain_ness: Legendary paragon Captain Ness applies boarding effect to entire fleet.

ship_designer.description: Interface for creating, updating, and customizing ship templates and defense stations. Designs cannot be edited while ships of that design are under construction or upgrading.

auto_generate.description: Computer designers continually update ship designs based on latest technology and selected ship role. Does not account for enemy compositions.
auto_generate.warning: May choose statistically superior components that enemies can counter.

cost.description: Build cost is the sum of all section and component costs. Build time determined by ship type.

upkeep.component_upkeep: 0.833% of build cost in Energy, 0.15% in other resources

upgrading.description: Ships upgrade to the most recent design with the same name. Retrofit button switches to a different design. Deleting a design marks all ships of that type for retrofit to the most recently changed design.
upgrading.cost_formula: Difference in build cost between new and old design. No refund if new design is cheaper.

power.description: Generated by reactor and Reactor Booster components. Many components require power. Designs with negative power cannot be approved.

ship_size.description: Ship size equals naval capacity and command points. Some weapons deal additional multiplicative damage based on target's ship size.

mechanical.description: Available to empires with mechanical shipset. Alloys for construction, Energy/Alloys for upkeep.

offspring_variant.bonuses: +50% hull/armor/shields, +25% weapon range, doubled cost and upkeep, additional Offspring Oversight core slot

ember_variant.cost: Fixed in Crystallized Entropy, unaffected by components

biological.description: Available to empires with biological shipset. Food + Alloys for construction, Food for upkeep. Three growth stages instead of sections.
biological.offspring_variant: Same bonuses as mechanical offspring variant

hard_cap.description: Limited number per empire. Mechanical and biological versions are statistically identical except cost.

defensive_structures.description: No evasion or disengage chance. Deep Space Citadels have fixed cost/upkeep unaffected by components.

mechanical.unlock: Cosmogenesis ascension perk + required crisis level + mechanical shipset
mechanical.loss_condition: Submit to Keepers of Knowledge demand to renounce aspirations

biological.unlock: Cosmogenesis ascension perk + required crisis level + biological shipset
biological.loss_condition: Submit to Keepers of Knowledge demand to renounce aspirations

menacing.description: Available to Galactic Nemesis ascension perk empires at required crisis level. Cost and upkeep fixed, unaffected by components, reduced by percentage modifiers.

nanite.description: Available to empires with Nanotech traditions. Cost and upkeep in Nanites, unaffected by components.

ship_sections.description: Each ship/station has 1-3 sections with weapon and utility slots. Changing sections resets all component slots. Weapon slots trade at fixed ratios: 1 XL = 2 L or Hangar = 4 M or Guided = 8 S or Point-Defense.

auto_design_roles.description: Auto-generate can be assigned ship roles for specialized designs. Stealth variants add cloaking and use only armor if cloaking device is available.

components.description: Individual systems defining ship capabilities: weapons, defenses, hyperdrives, utility systems. Most add to build cost and upkeep.

core_components.description: Right side of ship designer. Most cannot be empty (exception: FTL Drive, but required for inter-system travel). Titans and Juggernauts have an additional aura slot.

weapon_components.description: Top of ship designer, separated by section. Slots can be empty.

utility_components.description: Bottom of ship designer, separated by section. Slots can be empty.
